[{"Name":"kiran kotecha","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWjtctnJPq0KuypsGkpG3Q5HGnQ43l2PIoPF-phZHggB6tiL0UfDQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":3,"Description":"Shree Thaker Bhojanalay Thali is well known for its thali and there’s always a long queue, with an impressive variety of dishes served. The staff are lovely, and the place is clean and well maintained, which I truly appreciated.

However, I found the food lacking in taste — it felt like food just to fill the stomach rather than something to really savour. The dishes were served quickly, but if not eaten within minutes, they would turn cool, which affected the overall experience.

At ₹700 for a thali, I expected much better flavour and consistency. Perhaps people go more for the experience and the unlimited servings — the couple next to us seemed to eat enough for three people each!

It was an interesting one-time experience, but not impressive enough for me to return.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3hcubFzci2Jny1zuSy2j7XTvXykRTdf8oYFFrg0ot5_uRmW5wuI1oQINkyx6p03MJzcoESoELP77B9OiyW9KlfcqYCIj8b5Ellx2ozvjHmlNirxJZthUqYxTPchbYUt_-5gFXcVq_ofnOZ=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1vBEiY__oXDLevu0x38r3kWYL_EVVPEDj9VuF4rkbklzMRZLSMIWp_vFsMF3Yuf1IwOUDuKoCXp0NC0eJznfWwqrb30jl-fiROeucB6m6u2GvCLhTL415OGcSOSPgVKPKguDUHB0dGrLHJ=k-no"],"When":"2026-2-25"},{"Name":"Stringzzz","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUIrIHOFbGu18mawCMKuMKWWwqBoufgt9lfl8uCR5WLwL2K-t8=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Shree Thaker Bhojnalaya – A Timeless Taste of Authentic Gujarati Thali

Shree Thaker Bhojnalaya is one of the most iconic and beloved places to experience authentic Gujarati cuisine. Known for its traditional thali, warm hospitality, and consistent quality, this legendary eatery has earned a special place in the hearts of locals as well as visitors. Dining here feels less like eating at a restaurant and more like being welcomed into a Gujarati household.

The moment you enter Shree Thaker Bhojnalaya, you are greeted with simplicity and warmth. The interiors are modest, clean, and functional, reflecting the traditional values of Gujarati culture where food quality and hospitality matter more than luxury décor. The seating arrangement is organized, and despite the heavy rush during peak hours, the staff manages the crowd efficiently.

The highlight, without any doubt, is the Gujarati Thali. The thali is a wholesome and balanced meal that represents the true essence of Gujarat’s culinary heritage. It usually includes a variety of rotating dishes such as shaak (vegetable curries), kadhi, dal, rotli or puri, rice, khichdi, farsan, chutneys, pickles, and traditional sweets like shrikhand or mohanthal. Each item is freshly prepared and served hot.

One of the most impressive aspects of Shree Thaker Bhojnalaya is the unlimited serving system. The staff continuously serves food with great attentiveness, ensuring that guests never feel rushed or restricted. The flavors are authentic—mildly spiced, well-balanced, and comforting—making the food suitable for all age groups. The use of fresh ingredients and traditional cooking methods is clearly reflected in the taste.

Historically, Shree Thaker Bhojnalaya has been serving Gujarati food for decades and is considered a cultural landmark. It has preserved the original style of Gujarati dining, where meals are meant to nourish both the body and the soul. Many regular visitors associate the place with nostalgia, family gatherings, and cultural pride.

Cleanliness and hygiene are well maintained throughout the restaurant. Plates, serving areas, and dining spaces are kept tidy, even during peak hours. The staff is polite, disciplined, and well trained, adding to the overall pleasant dining experience.

The pricing is reasonable, especially considering the quality, quantity, and authenticity of the food served. It offers excellent value for money and is often recommended as a must-visit place for anyone wanting to experience true Gujarati cuisine.

Shree Thaker Bhojnalaya attracts a diverse crowd—families, elderly guests, tourists, and food lovers from across India. Despite the popularity and long waiting times during weekends, the management ensures smooth operations and quick table turnover without compromising service quality.

Overall, Shree Thaker Bhojnalaya is not just a restaurant but a celebration of Gujarati tradition, flavors, and hospitality. If you want to experience a soulful Gujarati thali prepared with authenticity and served with warmth, this place is an absolute must-visit. It leaves you with a satisfied heart, a happy stomach, and a deep appreciation for Gujarat’s rich culinary culture.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1t5xoGmbsoQ1wJYUFeRf1zpkGgWMD9UIqHNx2_nDEI3Bl-szlON-T5YAlE8N66zHQxmyZ8xnD-Rb8DQrJb96vLCXqAiAgIwqHag-RmqNonUjjuXliuSi2D_uQ7Pj3ahzXzlmsoQ2IF6oM=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn38hBd3BuUJ-zbZ9iwjwNLIuVcRaLTmal7HVCGC9SUB8mrrKBG3yOVTgXBzVCTnOIupvQkftekGWCwaeDay75Z_0oK5BlqeuIyS6LFn2s7OpbSxWBqpERf666DtxmWodYRSLN8AR6QLJFHs=k-no"],"When":"2026-1-15"},{"Name":"SATYA KUMAR Kondapalli","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV3rh6xMM0vKC5ENKnQe1BVYXKHQSmi_PRFNCR8f09lQF7exxOm2Q=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nice Rajasthani cuisine served with utmost love and affection. • Food: The quality, quantity, and taste were all spot on. Every dish served was fresh, flavorful, and satisfying. It felt like a perfect balance of fine dining with generous portions.

• Ambience:• For those who love scenic beauty, I’d recommend going during the daytime to enjoy the panoramic views of the city.
• If you prefer a more magical vibe, the nighttime experience is equally stunning with beautifully decorated lights adding to the charm.

Overall, SkyDining in Thane is a must-try for families, couples, or anyone looking for something beyond the ordinary. You get to dine 120 feet above the ground with a beautiful view. It’s a great place to celebrate birthdays, anniversaries, kitty parties, get-togethers, DJ nights, or even corporate meetings.

We tried the lunch (2pm to 3pm) which costs ₹2499 per person (GST included). They also offer different experiences like:

Sky Ride (4pm to 4:20pm) – ₹999
Joy Ride (5pm to 5:30pm) – ₹1499
Sunset Mocktail (6pm to 6:45pm) – ₹1999
Dinner slots: 8pm to 8:30pm and 10pm to 11pm – ₹2499 each

From the location aspect one of the best places in Mumbai. You can even sit outside on a terrace and enjoy the view over Powai lake.

We tried the Sunday Brunch which was really good. The focus of the buffet is Indiean Cuisine but yes you find some pasta dishes, sushi, momos, dim sums and fresh seasonal salads. For all dessert and sweet teeth this is the place for you. This place offers one of the biggest dessert varieties I experienced in Mumbai. Amazing.

We were there at 12:30 and this is a great time as it's not crowded and all is fresh cooked.

The stuff is friendly and they try to assist you with everything. They even guide you along the buffet and explain the dishes.

Prices are on the high side. Buffet plus two bottles of water 2 person EUR 105,--

Out of all the experiences it's a great location and if you like Indian Food it's a great choice but I personally have visited places with more vegan, glutenfree options.

I definitely will come back to enjoy a good cup of coffee in that wonderful calm Highly recommend the pesto pasta, Pizzas and the desserts from the buffet — both were fantastic! Special thanks to Shivam, Nidhi, and Tanmoy for the wonderful hospitality and an unforgettable experience.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1akgU_1kRM-t_MGvwtBjZ_ucLa0M5g372A5LKQWXsEbnxbQILi_Mjkrc0mntcskdLLajU_CQZ06purAjhrIQ_-UuhTiR_H2ke9GD32e2tSx0qarL5A1vp886N8VzbWkJ4i0TUxdewHURHC=k-no"],"When":"2026-2-9"},{"Name":"Deep Sett","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUT_EnA-QLsUBp95JoKOH-UW2jJJ0bd8TUL57QlA8JoUNRUo4u-=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"TOTALLY AVOID WASTING RS 5000.

At the onset this is probably one of the most expensive Buffets you would be having in Mumbai, priced at over 4300 plus taxes for a Dinner on a Weekday for an adult. Add to that, even as a gold accor member you probably get 20-30 percent instead of the normal 50 percent for 2 people is taxing.

The spread is decent, the variety in principal is there, but in terms of the taste and the quality of food, it’s really one of the poorest of the five stars , even considering the Leela and Lalit brands in Mumbai whose lay is very modest compared to this.

At almost 5000 you expect
1. Your veg sushi not being dumped in the refrigerator for you to pick up, questioning when it was made !

2. No juice , cold drink or mocktails are part of this offering. An advise to the team, you’re charging 5000 offering a drink probably at times could feel better than half the items on the menu.

3. I have seen just one Langarwaali daal the last 4 times I have gone here, to be honest , a Dal Makhani is the norm, if not that a top of the line dal Tadka.

4. Some dishes are borderline tasteless and on the menu just to increase the offerings on paper / website
A. Sushi - cold, refrigerated, and tasted as if it was made with zero interest.
B. Pizza Margerita- looked like an Udipi version not one served at a top 5 star, see the pictures yourself.
C. Fattoush salad, I mean if anyone has had a look at what they serve with that name, it’s embarrassing.
D. Starters like paneer tikka seem like lying there unattended for hours.
E. Many of the deserts too seem just good looking and tasting on borderline just sugar toffees.

When you come to a place , the stature of a Sofitel From the ACCOR elite stable, you expect basic standards and taste but unfortunately that’s missing.

The fact that it’s in BKC and most people have very little options for a 5 Star experience, plus because of its location the occupancy is high, is the best answer to why it’s half full during these buffets otherwise as per the taste of the food it should be  probably amongst the slowest moving places ever.

High recommend for a nice buffet experience if you have enough space in your stomach.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kunal Shah","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV0EFcpnVrkxeXSsb5U9fSAwNBTdWfQNDJ03nBCEoK3aa8URgmdlg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"We visited Pondicherry Café for dinner om our anniversary with an additional couple with fairly high expectations, especially given Sofitel’s reputation and our own positive experiences across Accor properties. Unfortunately, this turned out to be one of the most underwhelming buffet dinners we’ve had in a long time.

To begin with, the vegetarian selection was extremely limited, which itself was disappointing for a premium luxury hotel. What made it worse was that several items had already run out by around 9:30–10 PM, which is surprisingly early for a dinner buffet. The veg sushi, in particular, was tucked away inside the refrigerator with no proper labelling or indication—it genuinely felt like a game of hide-and-seek unless you already knew where to look.

We were excited to try the chaat and gol gappas (puchkas), but this was hands down the worst part of the experience. The chaat was served cold, soggy, and overly sweet, even after we specifically requested mild spice. The gol gappas were shockingly plain—filled only with boondi and nothing else, completely missing the usual exciting mix of chutneys, potatoes, or flavours.

The Indian vegetarian main course was mediocre at best, lacking both variety and flavour. The only true highlight from the Indian section was the freshly made garlic naan, which was genuinely good. The hot dim sum station was another pleasant surprise, as those were freshly cooked and nicely done.

A special mention must be made of the pancake counter—the lady handling it was warm and sweet, and the fact that the pancakes were eggless won us over completely. Though pancakes aren’t a typical dinner or dessert item, this ended up being the most enjoyable part of our meal.

Service suffered due to severe understaffing. The staff, though polite, seemed unsure of menu details themselves, and the small dining arena with closly sat tables, which further affected the overall experience.

Overall, this dinner experience was a 5/10 at best. As Accor Gold members who genuinely love Accor properties, this was by far one of the most disappointing dining experiences we’ve had in recent times. Sadly, we wouldn’t recommend Pondicherry Café for vegetarian diners and do not see ourselves returning anytime soon.

[{"Name":"Jyothis Thaliath","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUF5K19G-o_-5N3wLRFYaDwLXENRR4TkhGXa2XlSktvdCCMVJxz=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"We were planning to visit JW Mariott at Juhu as it has a reputation of having really good ambience. The fireballs that greet you at the gate and more at pool side, everything screams luxury. The interiors are very well done, suits a 5 star hotel. Only thing they didn't allow restaurant guests to visit the poolside which was a terrible loss.

We went for dinner buffet at Lotus Cafe. It was 3200+ taxes per person and free for kids below 5 years. The selection of dishes and live counters were good. But I think they lacked popular starters like Chicken Tikka or Tandoori. It was more towards continental style with grilled fish, etc.

The live pizza is a must try! The pasta has lot to improve, as I've had better pasta from cheaper joints. The kathi rolls were not exciting. I'd suggest them to add momos or dumplings as it is better suited to the menu.

The dessert section was overwhelming with icecreams, pastries and Indian Sweets like Rasmalai, Kaju roll, etc. It was all very good. However I would ask them to avoid multiple cakes and give some variety like Jalebi, cupcakes, eclair, etc.

On service front, it was amazing with very attentive and cheerful staff who caters to every need. Considering the cost, I would not recommend the buffet, but you can definitely come to dine. Best suited for evening with a good view of the pool.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn19n2ZRidgmw0ndIaLkQ9YuBq8YjEuaNKhdu3GzMg_mW_bXQpYlEBCevFp84kmArgoN9StXfGLjP3_UtQRXVjjoEIIGebuNs6SEe3NIkpR8AH60f10z4jX4EvQcQvbuvCJ_gtaPcr3uvGs=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1kjC9Yp8PmtnoXPqZC4wM9CNRTaCH-lxvNjK0BDGo5trKVQj25Mq7gF0gTOrmTkOm7aN2KCuD0z9aYspgIIYbIVAtYmdTZZcPnDujDacD3agc6XZVw8X9h4Xsm_JBzOB07hnA-wHIRYhQ5=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1dDgSrcb_Y5VApIL8nJ_1jxFdKdGF7K-CvPmFoAmN8iI-6X6QxYtDEvULsFe4nn6frEMHqBIENGmvdlBBH6j8S1I16_7Msoy7Wgy9K4GiUa71XxwwcJQSYQESl3yM58JAPCRVWOPGQFnuJ=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3gjiqRH9LEYA0-TljM3bbeTFd7XwJs0dQQ6QZ2qeLsBfp3tqjDmz1YBHSPdmAUsdFUz1wI1QjIBMRWLqGA9lxzeic212L-Ddt5KfGih9AhPeAGKzDYzPW-j_-HcSH96km5YMn6Sy0Hl7E=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2WGjcJxAJm7oATM827NTJuPVHWdaMRRJQiM8CQShESA17D3YU5AEIe94A6mZ8mQjxDUjYpr0FTT3RJqoanPkn8-ACAp-kLTA_2_0PeL_Btv51-6nnc8voDEPZnOfZ4T7t4Sb_02KEmoK4h=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn15Nm29iHUGkCUIgajUKqq4NdMPLeaJhZ6N_lh6iIasMsbOZK1egkeGBjdog0YAsZvlpvyzqa9KocgFTx7HPLppgJ5fshTe6jXpmrzuBEINf6XLAs8GoFarnfFPLDL2idfEdu3mBzgYciaj=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0tUfCxGs4Z1G__KfvyC7LsM9_NNwhPsdUJ8PTwk5AmlgzdTsLGJsxmDDqXruRbmJ-L3tLvSWQ6rPcWlY9gROR1kAVXYgn1ZVDiKrn7wPTLqJohUhc_W23ukCGPD3u75EYYq62KW9IbWqLZ=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1w8mLqLaAL6lAGJqJqMbGh4r0KlHFtzpWotYtdlWsmNZilIBpJdXT49EXQfAsvfnZyj_H_2Bhu-3Jsmdso5D7lL4UzYRO4135wYHYz1NnbZe9eXp7v8IppuXXSEkZ71a5CwSkq_O2q7qXt=k-no"],"When":"2026-1-25"},{"Name":"akaramch","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWDIngzv8eMSYqYbG_ynEtnfgh3CSx2dWL_CHGfHrS3oY1JPmaE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I booked a large table to take my family out for a nice lunch buffet. I’ve been visiting this restaurant 2-3 times a year for the last 5-7 years, and earlier, the taste they delivered was gold. Back then, they really tried to include everything in their buffet, and the dessert section used to be huge and impressive 🍰🍮.

Now, let’s talk about the breakfast buffet—it’s pretty standard, similar to what you’d find at any 5-star continental breakfast 🍳🥐. They do include a few Indian dishes, but honestly, breakfast here has never excited me since the beginning.

As for dinner, it used to be something I truly looked forward to because of how flavourful and varied the food was 🍽️. However, over the last 2-3 visits, it feels like something is always off—sometimes it’s the salt, sometimes it’s the flavours. The food feels same-same now, and perhaps I’ve become fed up of eating the same dishes repeatedly. There’s nothing new or exciting anymore, and the taste is not extraordinary enough to crave anything specific. I used to love the pizza here; it was one of my favorites. But lately, the taste has literally deteriorated 😞. It’s just not the same anymore, and it’s disappointing to see the quality drop. Hope they bring back the original flavors they were known for.

That said, this place still shines for its ambience ✨, and for newcomers, it might still feel impressive and worth the visit. But for me, my interest in visiting Lotus Cafe is slowly fading away 😕. I guess it’s a matter of having it too many times.

ago, for their famous Sunday brunch. I was spoilt for choice. They have a wide array of dishes to choose from.

I started off with their Asian dumplings and they were good. I absolutely loved chicken Sumai.

Next up, I tried a cheese platter with Gouda, Brie, burrata, cheddar, caramelised figs and crackers. Everything paired so well.

They had a wide range of cured meats, smoked tuna, smoked salmon and lots and lots of grilled veggies.

The lobster and oysters and tiger prawns were outstanding. Their mutton chops were delicious too.

But their pizza and drinks stole the show for me. The pizza base was perfectly chewy and charred while the cheese was melty and gooey. Their pineapple Pina colada was sweet and creamy.

The pasta was okayish. And some dish were just average.

They also had a wide range of dessert to choose from. But their Gulab Jamun and chocolate caramel dessert was overly sweet. But I loved their blueberry cupcake and chia seed pudding.

Two of us went there to celebrate my wife's birthday.
We had done prior booking for Sunday evening.     We were able to choose our table. Our server Krishna nicely explained the menu options. Since we were not that hungry, we settled for a la carte.
We had veg soup Satwar ka Shorba, Murg Barra  Kabab and Hyderabadi Murg Dum Biryani. Kababs and chicken from the biryani were succulent and cooked to perfection. Though it was Hyderabadi biryani, it was not spicy for us. At the end we topped it with kulfi.
Overall good food. Full marks for the service. Don't forget to check the discount beforehand.

elegant place I have ever been to. It has such a royal ambience. The staff is very friendly and supporting and they even have great knowledge about the food and they provide great recommendation as well. They have a soft music played in the background which truly sets the mood. The seating area was so comfortable and luxurious. They have a separate bar section, and they have separate seating in the middle, which is the most beautiful interiors I have ever seen. The FOOD was truly delicious, it was not a regular meal experience but an extraordinary meal experience. We had the Set 6 Course Non Veg Meal here which consisted of Soup, Appetizers, Mains, Biryani and Dessert. The classic items I tried here were:

SOUP
CHILGOZA SANTRA SHORBA : The soup was basically of root vegetables and it was served with malta.

APPETIZERS
1) AWADHI MURGH TIKKA/SARSON MAHI TIKKA: This dish has two items in the plate one was Awadhi Murgh Tikka which was Chicken marinated in Awadhi spices cooked in Tandoor and second was Sarson Mahi Tikka which had Fish Tikka marinated in Mustard.

2) CLAY OVEN DUCK WITH AVTAR-E-HALIM : It has roasted Duck served on a bed of Halim.

MAIN COURSE
NALLI TAR KORMA WITH TAFTAN/DUM KI KABULI DAL : The Royal plate had Lamb kofta with awadhi spices and black lentils dal served with taftan and assorted breads.

BIRYANI
JHINGA DUM MASALA BIRYANI : It was a royal Prawns Biryani cooked with Lahori Spices.

DESSERT
MISTHAN ROOH-E-UMMRAO : In dessert we had beetroot ka halwa served with kulfi. It was very delicious and a great way to end the meal.

The food was just mind-blowing and the best j have tried. It was truly a very royal experience of visiting this place. I'll surely recommend everyone to try this place DWwjbfIp0-2Vg86782_Gh2g3QzfdoZlPaf70jMvYd6p9SYgAaZuJanylul2Nz7pQMmf6bKhQvB6PvTNmNDYFHtDScMuMutFjhll5Jrgt0ebjIvry9R0Z_r=k-no"],"When":"2022-7-13"},{"Name":"Mohammed K","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKv9ZNpXkILnmGTh_ZALDQ9LFqZ06Ef1rLqJ0M_vL1sRzcK8w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Went for a dinner here on Sunday night ..Located in Courtyard Marriott you don't expect anything less than exquisite ...and the overall experience stays true to the brand name
The place has soothing ambience with a smiling staff .
The theme is based on royalty with mughlai dining experience and the menu is detailed , well iterated.
Coming to the food ..I went for a set meal ..It a complete meal trail right from soup till desserts .Here's what I tried
Brocolli Soup - Opens up your appetite , soothing and blissful in this climate
Jaituni Murgh and Rawas Tikka - Soft , moist , well marinated , appealing plating and taste to perfection ..Good start
Halim - Too good ..well grounded wheat ..you don't feel any lumps here ..smooth as cream topped with charred meat ..Must try ..
Murgh Do pyaaza - Brown gravy , rich and aromatic ..chicken tender and well settled with gravy
Kabulidal - I haven't had a more creamy and delicious dal than this one ! This is star ..a simple dish taken to a whole new level
Dum Biryani - The best part about this is meat and when I spoke to the chef..he said the meat is brought from Rajasthan and therefore you get the lucious taste ! Well cooked and well presented
Beetroot Halwa and Tart - Both the desserts are amazing .Tart was well made with Rabri and cherries on top !Amazing fusion
For Drinks I tried Noor , Sukoon , Mint Mojito ...Noor is a must try
Prices are quite competitive when you look at the overall quality , service , presentation and ambience !
It's a good place to celebrate something special and seems in the budget .Be sure to book the table before you go !","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3gqm0vyprLIvB7rhnxezA1P0iTKGGigwwHjR52Ez7aH3WnSaTr-MS5NFRgDgySV1qF9VkpC4lMp1XqLkeg8rfvsDX3qD1H37x_J7bcCBGgyqhpjYdWiHqAlLQOa6B2R6BW1IH9=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3CfLvVYH4bYgrjTgHgeh9TQz0O0e_VVvjj1uhft8UngJSqPMeZQVeNZOcyx6Pr9P3ttzjl9B3dRhXNenvieuvZ0Dvti-XVBlIbWPv7yhG0fpGukiWmS6FMEtSY1iSKd4EuaYoq5w=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn19apkIzzgy3Nf1b0o0Fswg4hqQkys5vljEKWzCsD7lBoQvlrSjqUaVGkVu7CpErX7-7bry7SYuRuxb-9_dzyMuZadZ0cvf7_ftg2fp5qajq--pUOl2MYwuPWIszOIywInijh8=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn28obfg7LrAS6dptKOpwQbLEXI26AYeYS28cxgKciItKZDzoun0RX1ryBHmnd0DYv0l3MIPBpDDmQ1zc8htnY2bam6Jgd8Z6ebXDrMA6ekUr30844j9hvAKu3hfJ2ryfqNROhGL=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0jaEoyZdXX9ty17u-R76NSrDscftHZoKgdr8fuwZxjxoAtBKR7B0R-Cxvq1Vew_L6BKsjcU_pNgNio6ewsWnP6nswvNrujia8IwINMp85rwMHRJDpCEp4wgoWNYNaZFLLKmaP3=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1faEO_jbON_D4mC9l4N7YQvTRfh22qy8nsm2DHvchOdM74P9y-8V4o8zamoQiHCL0tg3cQbvreY0n0t9H0jSGAuqkyriCnqKPnZWClsBuxU7AM6OEB2d2xyNFOOA4qUS-bWOEF=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3gogVjBK4ayVCmWTWFvIq-SY8ABHs_Of-VJSvjRl3pSW2PeRdI-pWPQKxc4RDUkw76O0k1asxeGZ6NDHs0Fwck9z1DQf2B3QsZ9uj2cw4asp6HranD-k-UmtRO4ZoELjYbB_I=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0yXHH5MZVW_qnrCQTS_uvT3f96CSNqt3W0E8EGjd1idLE-lf1uj_CYWeYWAj4cqXsstcV_xPHIA8gQwxNBr3dybvVsfQ_dE3MbI713o7xLdzjhB-rpj_LDRCefJ7Tu4v5nxnFJ6g=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0mNUnJiRMWUlseCGqrr088kXjdXnbFbGHED3rMy8Q4btEwy7HpTUy1mx-AevkUhm4P7Lzf-8PPi5tdrLfoZgeGtpXZ6lEThSM99JRGRKQ-YlcgZDMnuaRyivb7QeDASfynWkb3=k-no"],"When":"2021-12-20"}]

[{"Name":"Nagaraj Keshav","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKObSGjTrN5mAGX5NiN-sMp4R02yWNEzpPgI_lxYQoxDlBQ5tE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently dined at Shamiana in the iconic Taj Mahal Palace, Mumbai.

The food quality was truly impressive. Each dish was well-prepared, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The menu offers a refined dining experience that lives up to the reputation of the Taj brand. The ambience of the restaurant is equally commendable — elegant, warm, and comfortable. The overall atmosphere makes it a wonderful place for both relaxed meals and special occasions.

However, the service experience was somewhat average and did not entirely match the standards one would expect from a luxury property. While the staff were polite, the food was simply placed on the table once it arrived, and we had to serve ourselves. This was a bit disappointing, especially considering the premium positioning of the restaurant. Even many smaller restaurants ensure proper table service, so naturally, expectations are higher at a place like this.

Overall, Shamiana excels in food quality and ambience, but there is room for improvement in service standards to make the dining experience truly exceptional and aligned with the prestige of the Taj Mahal Palace.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1BZ0IKIuL2uKfF4plIfwKfBLZ5eVNNEhWgkMSfyxR4Kjt7hLeSCfgSv1M4TzCGj-WYrRmjcv0pbOw6gLccOsZyeAx_2_AgfNwiG3cJyqH-0ij1TsWy-XoVHX8JDjhzKmvg5g9DX761ql0=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2jlhKoZtaSfWstAluPa37-pGjTRbHwLmXVubqBz6loWVvxWP3DEQM2Qkc3Ru1L5cD1UkWes0W3ETunoMeVaG8YPrQSzTXW86kwF12qLxAbCRXHpUeKE_CWHMPROi5m4S5IVi2P_GUunBmP=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2fHSA2gIbhUZqKJZlkxEggsr2pRZR0RbifuX4qw19cSfW7dqBedN2ZKTQOpMzA5UPxDJ_jyYpmmwQs9JrdpKd83qPe-pFiJ0A4c0BoRR_ckNFIn9fnXi5Qp3Q4U39dQv6M0Njm29yStnhU=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2q9Xb-dSFW-FK5OizO0bjI3HKy53FSdGu3_Ui-2WOXS5Ty-MXRpdlRD9EqBLwocQrC51UyO23j1NjDheJEV9KCHb-DtoZI0aQAtm_SvFAs3D-gVTEhYr7Id9nCaFovSuyWBCqtkQMG_skR=k-no"],"When":"2026-2-15"},{"Name":"CSB “The Road Tripper”","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJvrw_hM-ZGItS0rIIz_d6iu0F3HK9B7Auy--Qrg167SYoNHA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"It was a shocking experience wth a hotel having so much of reputation. Looks like it's all hyped only.

To my Surprise, the Dining table was trembling due to uneven table legs. Heard a lot about Taj's hospitality; all went in vein that night. How come the Resturant manager didn't notice this. It's a catastrophic error on his part.

No one to attend or guide - We were six people and went on a weekend recently. And post having half of the meal I saw waiters serving starters in a similar looking like pans to many.  I asked a guest, whether we need order this seperately or part is the buffet. They said it's part of the buffet. The waiter will serve you on your table as the first thing. I was shocked. I asked the restaurant supervisor about the starters and he said you didn't got it Sir ?? I was like what the hell. The lady who was assigned my table  then was called and she served it immediately. How can they expect guest to know about it.

The mocktail bottle  was lying empty in the buffet counter until I brought this to their notice. People were standing in queue waiting for the same. Not expected from a hotel like this.

Food quality and taste was decent. However, few items like lamb should be cut in smaller pieces for the ease of guest. [{"Name":"Anirban S (an1rb6n)","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKAscBjBU4-2u6t3QLiTv8MLDZ6JKimqsG9fw0Z1oawdtqJbQLKw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This is a hard place to review. I was constantly torned between small misses here and there, and then they had something to offer to compensate. Overall, in the price point, it was a brilliant place to have breakfast. Since we only had breakfast buffet, my review is restricted to it.
Food options are plenty. There is a lot of variety available. In fact you can specifically request certain tweaks in them too. But the options at times are just weird in terms of placement. For example artisalan breads did not have the toaster near it. Near the toaster (and I found only 1 toaster) there was no bread. Once toasted the only spreads available is butter and jam. But they have waffle station which has all the spreads available. This is the operational planning lapse. But at the same time, they were kind enough to serve the bread on the table when requested. And they cooked up and awesome egg benedict. Since I don't care about dosa, idly, vada etc. - the cold cuts, ham, chorizo, chicken were good. The mixed miso soup with prawns were amazing. But the only variety of chicken as dish was the indianized chinese version of it. It's not inherently bad, but suprising to me a bit. Marriott after all 😅.
But I understand they are focused on the needs of the people, who would want these generalized dishes. I didn't find almost anyone else even near the hummas, or the pork sausages. Safe to say the target customers and my appetite (since I'm from Kolkata) were heaven and hell apart. But the spread was well balanced. They had a really good pineapple juice. But they didn't have the grape juice or it was like it'll take some time. But the pastries were really tasty. But for yogart they only had epigamia which was odd.
Small gaps here and there, but at the price point on a Sunday morning it was a superb brunch. Food were tasty, plenty, warm and overall good. Not extraordinary, but good.
Note, they close the breakfast by 11AM mostly, so reach early if you plan to taste a lot of items. But definitely worth a revisit.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2HxRXOAzbEv6tIKHTfE9gdmjnWeqOAW4rZPhP43LLmv82H9SYpNNzJYMSBZ6hGWGifsSLpTPTC6U4sIkxott6dblCZKtfDPe-jpB8lXfY6BhyvW8my2OIOzGzvYfNCOULJeYZHHnBafPKU=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1mNlBQsLM7hjibWcunFTr2aiKdg-BenwOksqdfIn3J5E5oWKejrFRoccfU9zIgbE6udfT2gyxFHEYC7Y2jpcobKxbTg-AE3xDmQpROsgjYd5-Vrs-RI9A0dP3y7XbDtQi8RpgJhcCSCI0=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1v4GPC3loOtcSNW1L2nZCS1mczwIb4hudDnX_ZmRk133j63ivaqqjFcK7COE2p3Q4N6ZrfN3F5rUg1MwHLy_84sVgjCBEZuNg17_pPSOngD-ncGixM-l2X-k_86pFmAcDW632RdGu2Gl4=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1GoLGHPIJTTzqxRFJkb8mSj0wynD5unJViVvlS5RnNnOIL_ytDHXEw7uO9yXGBVyRBpX01ToUCSJd9CB0x3yupaySjt8e2LfeucbTIxKWCfCVRG569n1HDwB7aBiM_AlaOrUEGU668KaGE=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1ZOmVFCcsj4Ew1jnzClOvlxo1vtCyVFI83CWZblLJwuwFUGhI-qBXPegrnHHDmcVXSKuvTvFf59Tg0Tpj7sReQvEBC7qpg0vjNVAA-sVifFCsNu5EksukKKwtMxQffmyyUtNkjtp9aWwM=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0_95F9jS_KAz1bklaBIcvwPq2FuyngCEuq0xVTWM7BeaGmgQ-RGV5zFmu_BN9CNygvYUz8Ppm2P7AbQeE4eMrftF-wNDDUraHAxaBLDLUel2U8FkFzn9QjLfvlEVF7VbpESfPbG2rmFD0=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1rw27S2bf2DJSHN1a7vbPko7WXmwKoaF8KmebGO4EaEZTV0cK5aenWixZBFR_dDbphZRriwYnztTVwTZrgGRYQF7PzMj-v2SN7vkPmSRAtKsERsDZHethmx2wh1XNTPks_ciWHSvgMhBLR=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2Nit4Lcsac9U409ONavyfZGZfbkopEnZ_hz5b2o8CbMr0EYhEIIq5Zhmnr6JycidGQ6fIy4-_rt2q83G167ARwwHk6Q-rzoKCdXqnK4idrAgqRCX6DMplCuUZYNlbhPw6mmzJEeCCw8Qo=k-no"],"When":"2026-1-18"},{"Name":"Neha Mendon","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXrKDUIKHF8HE4ae0UkmhBeYfscy3KfEHVmeGSNUlLisRNMV9wE=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Just had an amazing spread at J W Cafe inside JW Marriott Sahar! Horrible menu planning. Being ex hospitality professionals we took a stroll and took a look of /at the buffet spread.
Everything seemed just ok !!!

But when every dish was tried and tasted  , nothing hit right to the palate.

Tastless, and some were literally off taste .

I mean if one is spending around 6k plus for 2 people for a dinner buffet, the least you could do is keep varieties , offer mutton on the main course but no...no varieties , infact itseems the headchef who planned this menu wasn't interested in tasting the laid out food...and just for the sake of it and cost consciously kept less options thereby making the guest unsatisfied for his/her desire to try out varieties .

No steamed rice was offered on the buffet, the mutton kebabs were not hitting the palate right, the cold cuts were tastless, no smoked salmon to offer on the cold cuts( every hotel offers the same), the malai cheese paneer was just dry and weird tasting, the green fish curry was just the biggest blunder on the menu. Being a weekend ( Saturday) and such pathetic buffet option is laid out then might as well shut down your buffet. ...better options are available with standalone restaurants in and around the vicinity.
Had come here to celebrate a birthday and was just left disappointed. Never coming back again for sure...

With regards to service , I must say the SERVICE  PERSONNELS were attentive to clearing the tables and refilling our water glasses. So kudos to the service team, but the kitchen team and its head needs to evaluate their menu and revamp the buffet menu.

Last but not the least...when it comes to food, if a guest is disappointed, the guest will definitely not come back again. so I guess you guys lost a customer .

The selection was very limited, and items that ran out were not replenished. There was no chicken curry on the buffet menu, and despite asking several staff members, none could provide an answer. After we had been eating for an hour, a chicken curry dish was quietly added. If it was unavailable earlier, they should have communicated that clearly. The chocolate brownie and Sabudana wadas were never restocked once finished. We have enjoyed far better buffet experiences elsewhere. Additionally, one of our relatives discovered a long thread in their Awadhi mutton biryani, prompting the chef to personally come over and apologize. This falls far short of what one expects from a 5-star establishment.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Veronique Eichler","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXbchZM5tiIHwHXpBzbbjd_Poz66Utcg7sL3Ik3cSgTVcH8OxgMrg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We had a very nice dinner at Peshwari. Great ambiance, delicious food. Our waiter helped guide us to some great menu selections.
A few notes on the negative side. They couldn't find our reservation even though we had a written email confirmation - turned out it wasn't a problem to get seated but the place ended up completely packed so it's a good thing we arrived early. Also, when our appetizers came out we had to ask for a fork and knife since there were none at the table. We had to ask 3 times before they were provided to us, and the manager's excuse was that these were really finger foods we should eat with our hands... And finally, the prices are steep, but I guess it goes with the class of hotel the restaurant is located in.

user_reviews:
[{"Name":"Raj Roy","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocI_OUoifPISWrfpBMm_rYJsNcQwWl9guVduKtPDqc5iqyKd4w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you want to experience service, this is the place you should visit. I had gone there for dinner on a special occasion. At the last minute I called them to request if they can arrange a candle light for the dinner just to make it more special.
Though it was just hours before Mr. Kiran was very kind to no only arrange a candle but also give us a complimentary cake.
Now comes a more detailed review:
1. Service:
I will rate them a 100/10. The service is simply out of this world. They are the best. They gave us a tour of the entire hotel from inside all the restaurants and bars and everything. By their service they proved that they are a 5 star hotel. Kudos to the team. I have clicked a pic of the team and uploaded in appreciation.
2. Food:
Food was good, variety was large spread, though it was non spicy food mainly to suit the pallate of foreigners who do not eat much of spicy food. They also had options of street food like pani puri, dal bati churma, etc.
3. Ambience:
Pretty good Ambience, nice place, you will enjoy it. The Sahara group has indeed done up the hotel very well. The crowd there is not a typical sobo crowd but good people. Humble enough. Mostly Indians who have come with their families.
4. Pricing:
Being a 5star it was one of the cheapest 5 star hotels that jbhave been to. I also opted for offers on swiggy so I got a good 40% or so discount on the total bill. Hence, I paid approx 5k for 2 people which effectively comes to approx 2.5k per person and considering this is a 5 star hotel, it's very reasonable for the price, Ambience, service, extra service, hospitality, etc.

Overall:

Celebrating the spirit of Onam in the grandeur of Sahara Star was truly a delightful experience. The plush property, located right next to Mumbai’s domestic airport, set the perfect stage for a festive indulgence. The lavish Sunday Brunch at The Earth Plate – Global Cuisine transformed into a traditional Kerala Sadhya spread, offering an authentic taste of heritage served with warmth and elegance.

The banana leaf thali came alive with a vibrant array of curries, pachadis, thorans, avial, olan, sambar, rasam, pulisery, and fluffy red rice – each dish bursting with homely flavors, perfectly balanced spices, and that comforting touch of Kerala’s culinary roots. Crisp papadams, tangy pickles, and steaming hot appams completed the meal, while payasam and other Indian sweets brought the feast to a heavenly close.

The buffet around the restaurant was equally indulgent, showcasing a harmony of regional Indian delicacies, global favorites, and stunning desserts – all beautifully curated in a festive floral setting that reflected the joy of Onam. The live counters, attentive chefs, and vibrant ambiance added to the overall charm, making it an immersive dining experience.

✨ From the soulful flavors on the leaf to the elegant hospitality and plush surroundings, the Onam Sadhya at Sahara Star was more than just a meal – it was a cultural celebration plated with luxury. F","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU4cLl4qt6V1SdNef6AATUPphINh4IbFOC2knxF3UGZjNa8s-6GLQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Once a legendary staple of Mumbai’s culinary scene since 1958, Gulshan-e-Iran appears to have lost the magic that built its heritage. The shift away from their famous buff seekh has left a noticeable void in the menu that the chicken alternatives simply cannot fill. A recent attempt at the Chicken Seekh Kabab and Tawa Paratha proved to be a major disappointment, lacking the flavor profile one expects from such an established name. While the Chicken Kheema in Green Masala offers a saving grace with its well-balanced, mild spices, the overall lack of variety in the tawa and grill sections forced a pivot to basic Dal Rice just to round out the meal.

Ultimately, this iconic spot now functions better as an emergency food option rather than a dining destination. Given its location in a high-traffic business market with limited surrounding choices, it serves its purpose for a quick, functional bite, but it no longer carries the "must-visit" status of its glory days. This establishment is a paradise for food lovers, perfectly blending flavor, quality, and service.
Their Special Falooda is, without a doubt, the best in Mumbai. It's a refreshing, creamy indulgence that instantly uplifts the mood. The perfect balance of rich kulfi, sweet rabri, sabja seeds.

The first thing that stands out about the place is its beautiful interiors — they are quite impressive and give off a royal, grand kind of vibe. The ambience definitely adds to the overall dining experience. They also had live music during the daytime, which created a pleasant and lively atmosphere.

The service, however, was average. The staff was polite, but the food preparation and service took longer than expected, so you might have to wait a bit for your order to arrive at the table.

In terms of food, I ordered the tandoori chicken. Unfortunately, it wasn’t the best experience. The chicken was slightly burnt on the sides and, considering the price, the portion size and quality didn’t really justify the cost. The place felt a bit on the expensive side overall.

I’m rating this place 3 stars mainly because of the ambience and the overall experience the restaurant offers. However, the food quality and pricing could definitely be improved to match the premium feel of the restaurants located in Juhu, surrounded by popular spots like R’Adda, Yeda Republic, and Jhatka. Despite the competition, Tanatan consistently manages to attract and hold the attention of the crowd, and for good reason.

Every aspect of this place truly lives up to its name. From the ambiance and décor to the overall vibe, everything screams TANATAN.

The space is beautifully decorated with different types of bells 🔔, adding a quirky yet cultural charm to the interiors. The live music makes the experience even more happening and lively, turning a simple outing into a memorable one.

The seating arrangement is quite comfortable, and the restaurant is impressively spacious,perfect for relaxed dining without feeling cramped. The staff deserves a special mention: extremely cooperative, attentive, and genuinely helpful, making the entire experience smooth and pleasant.
All in all, I had a wonderful time at Tanatan. It’s an ideal spot for families, large groups, and even date nights. The music and overall contemporary interior with fine detailing makes for a pleasant experience .The larger than life bells beautifully done up walla, cutlery give a royal rich vibe
The Oreo Milkshake was rich and creamy with the perfect chocolatey blend, while the Tanatan Sangria was refreshing with a well-balanced fruity kick with crunky apple

Zucchini Paneer Rollatini was a new take beautifully crafted with soft paneer wrapped in flavourful zucchini layers.
Sarsoon Broccoli had a lovely smoky char and a bold mustard marinade that stood out. The presentation was gorgeous

In mains, the Aloo Kulcha was soft, generously stuffed, and baked to perfection, paired with Pindi Chole that had deep, robust spice notes. Authentic flavours, great taste
The Rasam with Ghee Rice and Papadam brought a comforting South-Indian touch with aromatic ghee and crisp papadam.

For dessert, the Malai Rabdi was luxuriously creamy, and the  Gulab Jamun was warm, soft, and melted beautifully with every bite.

[{"Name":"Ronak Ahuja","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVgr4PTlIvtt_98yhgz3ipprTHQ6z-E8EG1blglhAs2tfno6Sfc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"So we recently were at the Sahara Star as my gf loves that hotel. And we felt like having Lebanese and there was no other better place to be but MABRUK.
I just have one thing to say. They have simply upped their game.
The seating is so cozy and ambiance is so comforting. The space is open and almost in the centre of the hotel and you can see the beautiful lighting of the hotel above while dining.
We started off with the classic Sheesha and it was a perfect start till we wait for our meal to arrive.
things we ordered : ( In ranking of the best )
1. Chicken Shawarma - This redefines what shawarma is and my god will always be my favourite in mumbai.
2. Kibbeh Lamb - Soft succulent lamb with herbs, fried to perfection.
3.  Lamb Kawarma - Minced Lamb roasted well served on a bed of labneh. Simply finger licking good.
4. Mezze Platter - Needs No introduction. It was spot on and we ordered it with the Zataar naan 🤌🏻🤌🏻
5. Batata Hara - This too was decent. No wow factor to it.
6. Mandi Rice - Aromatic Rice with nuts mildly flavoured topped with juicy tender medium spiced chicken which compliments the rice.
7. Baklava - We thought we were tooo full but when this came we left no crumbs. Again they make it Inhouse and it was lipsmacking.

The Jr Sous Chef visited us and he too was so warm and welcoming. It’s one of those rare restaurants that manages to feel both familiar and surprising every single time. The space has an easy charm — elegant but not trying too hard — and the energy always feels just right, whether it’s lunch sunlight filtering through the windows or a lively dinner service.

The food is consistently beautiful in its simplicity. Every dish feels like it’s been thought through — fresh, balanced, and built around great produce rather than fuss. You can taste the confidence in the cooking. The menu reads globally, but the soul of it feels distinctly their own.

And the service — calm, intuitive, quietly excellent. It’s clear that a lot of care goes into every part of the experience.

After all these years, The Table still feels like one of the most assured, grown-up dining rooms in Mumbai — the kind of place you leave already planning your next visit.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1ZrlZsl-LfOMFw5ku40hQGTbfjL3ohh30ujO1Q7753BzLyuhiQhYU14HHrvI3q46HPtQTIG7GVP-2PQKoMstr4tLGYaC3m26hxtCbkjcWoqkFkQY0VZ2dRkSM243ka1-uTH6yILJSNKp8=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3GlMmTZcotw-RBk5NWdMkMcFVZgFEQqwSaAIn0aHJ2BJ2_Vxduxe9yOQBR0WARMdc3DPDQ-v-WgIXqgVVLHdfsDOyO4E7IDLCBRpshiKNPSg-ZjMzJ21CtomymU0wslLsediQ0cnQz8OME=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1Eq1TByo7pdESROh7T3gtfngqJS0d6Y9Ms_aiKmJUvQTGa0omxAXSSbFI_0x46rsxr8wn1ua5LchCrl2y9y3nAZorQGQjN-ODWSTTFTTBfSmcT5Y3rkXCpW53q423aVxxeu-5iIRXFzQq3=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn16YKmkrMp1arXJ-3_7PMzVruRRxERdBcC3VrAZj3JBVtxnBXpjC0_Oi6-12yk-9Q49NkivSJaFXLHbG_0A0oxYSnN3timC_wq2db2HmrNIlFogHto-wrICbfbUObFx090bx55mWp54CKbN=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn12nycZrdEZ8ABl-wcJJ-uFUsEjFihnT2y7eLVz0_HRiMP84UltGX6TSK2GS3mHXNgFmgXVwklF2lMYomo-VnM2cq17CTxZbL1f_oIqqv32UCVUhEXQHVrGsv-negIxqRDHwdzIaDZAvzut=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1bRy1BfBX_6n2WbhZbfWBYkvYZb9kR5058F4lgD9OEwXH8I0MR092BJyXTUfkDP1KyR6aK4yEsBDD-MmZRf7D6GKAJiomuv4c14p_ku01QSmY6QeoYwZDbO3Q0FNgfvEWZDOqNDieySC2v=k-no"],"When":"2025-11-10"},{"Name":"Vishal Shah","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXA3L3QFPJZUbXzRR8rleNwkjUSQuN4YgEvHhqT8eTDUaUctKi7=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Really enjoyed our meal at The Table, Mumbai. He personally came to check if everything was good, helped us with the ordering, and gave excellent suggestions.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"vishal sawant","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVcvOsuiqR3enhkg0QV0yCOmanVmIOqMH8og4jnNaKqP_jGRW5rDw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"As the spirit of Ramadan fills the air, we found ourselves at Flavour Cottage, nestled in the heart of Bohri Mohalla, Mumbai—a haven for food lovers seeking authentic and flavorful delights. The place was buzzing with energy, packed with food enthusiasts enjoying their Iftar meals, and we were lucky to secure outdoor seating, which enhanced our experience amidst the festive chaos.

Ambiance & Service
Despite the massive Ramadan crowd, the service by Mr. Armaan was impressively quick. The restaurant efficiently handled orders while maintaining quality and taste. The setting, though simple, allowed us to immerse ourselves in the vibrant street food culture of Bohri Mohalla.

Food Highlights
We indulged in a variety of dishes, and each one left a lasting impression.

✅ Chicken Seekh Kabab – Juicy, well-spiced, and grilled to perfection. A must-try!
✅ Mutton Seekh Roll – Packed with robust flavors, the roll had a perfect balance of spice and tenderness.
✅ Chicken Malai Kabab – A standout dish! The kebabs were melt-in-the-mouth soft, creamy, and incredibly flavorful.
✅ Rumali Roti – Light, soft, and the ideal companion for the kebabs.
✅ Baida Roti (Chicken) – Crispy, stuffed, and full of meaty goodness.
✅ Mutton Biryani (Boneless) – The best dish of the night! The rice was perfectly cooked, infused with rich spices, and packed with tender mutton pieces. Each bite was a burst of flavors.

Overall Experience
Flavour Cottage truly delivered an exceptional Ramadan food experience. The quality, taste, and service were remarkable, making it a perfect spot for Iftar. If you’re in Bohri Mohalla, this place is a must-visit, especially for their Mutton Biryani, Roomali roti, and Seekh Kababs.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn232qpi5tfCYxd8b4fHWiGK7ilQz1hWtoxwMMQyAAeu54vnHpllMlMm0tzjvBHXD6VhbHbIpJt54qXqmdDF6m9ChkYMPProlCZ_OYzch1UaM5Tkk7Xk2rWwP28XctRNftwxWkZz=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3OCfmM286CexXXmmllm0FdMGBbuLfsH3DBXJBGC2oJFIPmr2LdtGcEu1uHrDOwm4cBIO5-_M4PjhaK4ChLIKGszRLd9xQ0GXUbIVqMEHQEg21BOEP7FEPRSmQ8eyp_VV2qLIFV=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3YSsq9DaEh99lWeXPcB-sSeziwsAIzd3Bv8TPj1Cov3dBfm4IP_QWdCTE7-sbPrvSRIKtTl07EqzlWBdZPVNv_Da_nPniVqOBpNKg0Zx4eVg3wcF75DmW0f1tjj_NX-NGqxZjrUw=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3hiLWahT3SBA_MDOj_1YPGcbFPkfXInlhIt7q21SsXux8VQJbO7_24_zqJZr0TKsDLALwLKt_bII0cU3Yd5ZhkNrIUuKfLAYAcYjBitnwlgvh16vly3yLo3eR-JPRGp_VaHWQ=k-no"],"When":"2025-3-23"},{"Name":"Razin Navrange","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJxQCZZY6PhmgSKGZLHOsozgsqNCZuM-sBYlTrJVU57uMubs4Q=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"⭐ My Honest Review ⭐

Pretty friendly staff generally.
Ambience is pretty good too, they had live music going on in the lobby, if that's your thing.

Food:
The spread was very good in terms of variety.
Soup was sweet corn, so hadn't tried it.
The salad section was very good, good options, great taste.
They had a hummus and cheese section. Good overall, but the breads accompanying it tasted a bit stale/dry.
The chaat counter was very good, everything I tried from there was fantastic.
Pasta - white sauce was mid, red sauce was decent.
They had a dosa live counter, all good there too.
Bao live counter - I'm personally not a bao person, so take this with a pinch of salt. I found it okay, less stuffing for my liking.
Asian items were the highlight for me, the stir fry was fantastic. Rice and noodles were great too.
Indian - the broccoli starter lacked significant flavour, tried a bit of the paneer jalfrezi which was okay. Dal rice was decent.
Desserts - the highlights were the pudding and the tart. Shrikhand was appreciated by my family too, however I personally found it a bit sour. they had a bunch of the typical run of the mill pastries which I avoided. The baked rasgulla was well below par. Three ice cream flavours, not bad, not great, nothing to complain.

Will I come back? I don't know, considering the options available in Mumbai.
Although definitely value for money with the Zomato carnival discount.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Harshad Dhamankar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXccUbEc3BRXZKcLsFHyKbdRS8B7w7xz9g4GgpSswaARdUTP905=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I have visited lot of times. Below contains reviews ans pictures taken multiple times.
Holi - The festival of colors. The same resembles to the food we eat. On this festive fervor, I stepped in to enjoy this magnificent colorful Holi Brunch.
As soon as you stepped @courtyardmumbai you will see huge dessert counter. But today my eyes captured all authentic glossy, classy, looking desserts and I was so amazed. Everything was looking stunningly colorful.
I started my Brunch with Khow Suey and varieties of starters. I love the Ragda Patis Chaat, Chhole Kulche, specially Chhole was made up with nice Punjabi style. Litti Choka was absolutely delicious. The sourness, spiciness and hint gave the perfect tongue twister. Potato Cheese balls were crispy and went well with green chatni. Ditto with Paneer, mashroom and babycorn Satay.
In main course I had various varieties amongst that Hyderabadi Biryani was delicious. Curd was not at all sour. The vegetables cooked with appropriate spices. Paneer Lababdar was definitely winner here.
Now my favorite part during the Brunch which, was Dessert. As I said I couldn't refrain myself from eating desserts at the earliest. I again selected Indian desserts and it was WOW!. Each and every dessert was made in uniquely. Different types of Gujiyas, Meetha Samosa were so delicious ditto with Jalebi and Laddu which was packed with lot of Ghee. The Coconut Rasgulla won my heart too. Meetha Paan was stuffed with dry fruits and had a Gulkand flavor to it. Very rare I see this unique dessert. I wish there couldn't have been any disease called Diabetes to repeat all those desserts multiple times. 😅
To accompany my meal I had Pina Colada (complimentary given) and Fresh Lime Soda. I had those to bring my taste buds back from the colorful food.
Service was great and I never had any complaints about service here. Everyone is on the tows ensuring there shouldn't be any delay.
So, I had a great Holi themed Brunch. Varieties of food but massive traditional dessert preparations bought more colors on my Brunch.

Dinner Time! I love dinner on weekend specially on Saturday that gives ample time to relax and eat everything on the table. And if I have such an amazing spread like this which offered @courtyardmumbai then it should be an elevating experience.
Starting from soup till the dessert I couldn't found even a single dish that was lacking spice.
Starting with soup and starter, this time Tomato Shorba was not thick but had spice to it. Chat platter was delicious and the curd used was not at all sour. Rest starters were good how ever I didn't eat Paneer Tikka more cos I had to save space for later course. 😅
I loved Punjabi Kadhi though gave a perfect spice along with aloo palak dry sabji. Veg Dum Biryani was packed with lot of flavors and for me it was spicy but the taste can be managed by adding curd. I didn't ordered roti this time I finished everything with plain rice and Biryani.
How can I miss desserts. I specially saved some space to grab those desserts. Balushai was so amazing ditto with Bengali sweet which topped with pomegranate and chocolate cream and dry fruits. Western desserts were good but I love the Indian desserts more.
At last I had fresh lime water to wash it down again it was not sour and bitter. Was rightly made balancing sweetness.
Alright, I enjoyed the dinner but being honest what I enjoyed more was main course and Desserts.

I will definitely be visiting again.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"hina ansari","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWKIDCrIotBGdoKFvgDmbbTLha2u8dsKkdFVKGCmdj_4ezq6hfgFQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"°°°ZYTUN°°°
Yesterday hit this place for dinner.  It's in marol.
Ambience is amazing. Above the restaurant there is Hotel.
It was raining outside and the dinner gave perfect moment to us.
So, we started with mocktails,

° Zytun special mocktail - it was mixed fruits based mocktail.

° Sunlight - it was orange - pineapple based mocktail. It was so refreshing.

° Tangri Kebab - It was chicken leg piece baked in tandoori, was looking tempting. It tasted good.

° Pahadi Kebab - I literally loved this. The color, texture,  taste.  Everything was just perfect. One should definitely try this.

° Murgh Maharaja -  it was in main course we order this with tandoori roti.
The gravy was thick and chicken pieces was more than enough. Taste was awesome.

° Fresh Fruit Salad - it was like normal fruit salad with ice cream on top of it. However presentation took my heart.

° Malai kulfi - its my favorite one.  I can't say NO to malai kulfi ever. There was chocolate syrup on it which was also impressive.

**Recommended**
° Sunlight.
° Pahadi Kebab.
° Murgh Maharaja.

Karkar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVseYfnElSRWavv-FT1-7tYQj8OTN3APn68yal7WNa1FmHffhyizw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Zytun - The Restaurant

You may find the road to reach the restaurant bit crowded and the parking space seemed a bit congested.
The restaurant is part of Hotel Metropolis Mumbai on Marol-Maroshi Road (Andheri).

Ambience: The ambience of the restaurant was good, the lighting was decent and the restaurant is medium sized.

Staff: The staff was polite.

Food: We had ordered the Chicken Manchow Soup. The soup was bit salty but it tasted nice with good amount of chicken in it. We had also ordered Chicken Clear Soup which was very well prepared. Following that we were served - Chicken Badami Malai Kebab. It was something new for us and we were trying it for the first time. It was cooked in cream and roasted almonds and it tasted very nice. Highly Recommended. And at the end, we had Chicken Tikka Biryani which also bit salty.

Crowd: Since we had reached the restaurant late (around 10 PM), the restaurant was less crowded. You would find mostly families dinning in here. There are very few good restaurants in this locality, I would recommend to contact the restaurant in advance for reserving table(s) during peak hours (for lunch & especially dinner).

Total Bill - RS 280 (divided amongst 3 people)

We ordered -
1) Chicken tandoori - 3/5
Taste was great but I've had better tandoori in other places. Small chicken pieces, not huge like a tandoori should be. The green chutney served along side was yum though and really enhanced the flavor of the dish.

2) Chicken Biriyani (RS 280) - 3/5
Tasty biriyani rice but a full bowl of biriyani will get you only 2 chicken pieces, which was not adequate at all. They serve you tons of rice but no chicken to balance it out to presumably save on their costs. It's tasty but if you're a chicken fan more than a rice fan, it's a mediocre dish.

Ambience - wonderful ambience. Very clean and neat, comfortable seating area, well lit, great pristine washrooms. Prompt service and we got our bowl of extra chutney whenever we asked for it.

Overall food - 6/10
Overall ambience - 10/10

Another thing to improve is the coordination between staff members—there was a noticeable communication gap while placing our order.

Visited The Food Studio near Nahur Railway Station and had a generally pleasant experience. We were seated within 5 minutes, and the ambiance was nice with a large TV setup in the center, adding a lively vibe to the place.

We started with French fries and a few starters: Jhinga Koliwada, Makhmali Kumbh, Pomfret Tandoori, and Chicken Burnt Garlic. The Jhinga Koliwada was the highlight—crispy and flavorful. Makhmali Kumbh is a great vegetarian option, stuffed with cheese and tasted quite good. However, the Pomfret Tandoori didn’t meet expectations—the masala lacked punch.

For the main course, we ordered Sabzi Peshawari with roti. It leaned towards the sweeter side, possibly due to the mix of paneer, mushrooms, and green peas. Not bad if you enjoy mildly sweet gravies.

We also tried the mocktail Mulokai under their Buy 1 Get 1 Free offer. The taste was average.

Overall, a good experience with room for a few improvements.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3pLbBrp0UF1EDwPLQMedwN0eQvZrvmexIGvLHF1RtopC-XgtGQu9P2arUcKQm8EHY58HfYdiE-cp3j0JdxER8HQZZ-LhZ2HXIgH187a_nOLwfTUH0q_4K2pUyDARn9bd-0XK63s4BtheyP=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn15EgQVA3B9zipD61V3wpAsMhxnsFBVjQiKWVJISwFfJoiDmKZDyVrjf0T-dgj1-kAYdOWBqXBAAqnq1M7IdFzpWTMbVAHfvLSQ6neAl391Lk4aJlH5MyhdaL6Suocw-SP94grB2AeZdCxC=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3_fl17VCSiwQYCu4e-Bvvp9G1phKa6ZqLUeqwHrhDMn5IB-R5Wd5T_czLbVBbCAJ-IHgCR0C3QtPeMornVesVpPBjCqFk_OLy5QrWMiJ3oLFUDeZzZXWzinBtJkFJUuNaq1f5e0P1dM9w=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn13AmiEjxOPp_0z0ntNpP6DwKJlfm4RhOfsdgS4BYI2AXC2vFyYcarZkiroyLtowdG4NsfApgLvV7eDt9ZKkxXainz4RjdcJvYwxiW3N6DHgK1epgaD3zvNqgVNqjTx9vLpbddG5xGSraV1=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3cRFSW8kpQ2gTYhieZKen5TZsbv1RiMgNpBpG1xnfMl_Nf8ZqQj0jCymG_zAn0BlmP7x0aXi8mjUnUF8rkC9QzjdXtu2CHLIBM4NVgOcOIA4049m1kRKxEAx-o6_F840MMmcZO3M4ajabl=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0B51Zc2KlHfcwhqHMnkjXssJjPCXzfxRpIpnOWwJdE9f0w5nU_seYlpunkz_UnIo3DlBe-_peMI4fTZzW92p3whXQJMYnepLPgd9IQdWZM6dTOdEmm4BSeunrlZYM4zwBSi2fs5mvFEC7s=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2jGiZoRfvmiFdnRtdAf-4IAet2O3mFsjMWv9J2lxU3YydtLc3hptxu-NhKPLwxYVYirAVpz4IEexkc3On7VzrIU9RVKmcXXgdDe8FsYdiX4M5enpwAzYWEhAJuKiPek_PT9fwVrELpm0Xb=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3Ccsk-wqBpPkph1RInR75A25GBv9w4EqeQ9QAW2VlzzY2dvA_TILc4O08SHpJqTYma7bjWXOdwc3gkNYBgTIhbqeU_W-Hn7i1Cy0nSJJPFTbLHex2fPBVuXQokSwq9LgzX--qIh2NsCI2U=k-no"],"When":"2025-7-26"},{"Name":"shweta apte","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUe0aimxfmfHXQMa7bnjxjQOmwsRaJtXhdFjj_fItLnIDSoO8taDQ=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"My family and I have been regulars at The Food Studio for over 14 years, and it has never disappointed. The food is consistently top-notch, and the service is warm, attentive, and thoughtful. Over the years, I’ve seen their menu evolve beautifully with the times while still maintaining the quality they’re known for.

They have several standout dishes, and what I appreciate most is their consistency—especially when hosting larger groups. We’ve celebrated multiple occasions here with 12+ guests, and each time they’ve delivered with care and attention to detail.

Most recently, I chose The Food Studio over places like The Bombay Canteen and Tanjore Tiffin Room for a family celebration—simply because of the quality, quantity, and diversity of food they offer. There truly is something for everyone, and they execute it well. Even their Chinese menu isn’t just an afterthought—it’s genuinely good.  A special shoutout to Mr. Sachin, Rajendra, and the rest of the team who looked after us during our party. Their attentiveness was exceptional, they anticipated our needs even before we had to mention them. That level of care truly makes all the difference.

Take my word and just go ahead and order any of these;
Appetizers
- Kali Mirch chicken kebabs
- Batter fried prawns in black pepper sauce
- Veg hara bhara kebab

Mains
- Railway mutton curry
- Punjabi butter chicken
- Chicken fried rice
- Chicken manchurian","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sushmita Shinde","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJkFkvfHGgK0KUQqisLtSd24r1LNxT7qKmAmp6c6DEAJTQmyg=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had a really great experience at Food Studio! The service was prompt and the staff  Rahul Kumar made us feel comfortable throughout. The quality of the food was impressive, everything was fresh and well-presented. Taste-wise, every dish was spot on and full of flavor.

The ambience is pleasant and perfect for a relaxed meal with friends or family. Overall, absolutely loved it and would definitely visit again. Highly recommended! ✨🍽️","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Khushbu Gosher","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocK4Cd32ScCeywN7tG9Sti0TbzbS-mANYtxNN3CBf8UKKkVo3g=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Wow, The Food Studio, just what I needed - a side of chicken with my veggies 🙄. Ordered veg, got chicken rice. Not just any chicken, but a full-on assault on my vegetarian and Jain sensibilities. Refund, apology, and compensation for mental distress = minimum expectation. Don't mess with a girl's food, guys.

[{"Name":"Harsha Shah","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXd_T7ZZWtSCI3IGhXWwefv6lBOF4M11VTwhV66vjeAKw1uljVCIg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I went there on 16 April 23. Simply amazing hospitality and such pretty restaurants. This Infact, was my reason to visit Nawab Saheb for their ongoing Tawa & Tandoor Festival (20th- 28th September) as there are very few restaurants who actually go out of their way to curate special menus like these. Brainchild of Chef Yunus Khan, I think everything on the menu was fantastic. Service was slow as the restaurant was quite packed, being a Friday night. I opted for the vegetarian fare priced at 2800. Frankly, I thought it was value for money only because of the main course. Just their dal makhani and paneer will leave you licking your fingers till the end. Here's what the spread consisted of and how I felt about it :
Vegetarian :
1. Til Malai ka Phool (5/5)- Melt in the mouth brocolli with cream cheese. Found the cauliflower okayish.
2. Chef Yunus Khan ka Paneer Tikka (10/5)- Star dish! Has to be the best I've had in a long time. Highly recommended.
3. Subz ki Ashraphiya (3/5)- Found it average.
4. Bhune Bhutte ke Seekh (5/5)- AMAZING combination of seekh Kebab and babycorn. YUM.
5. Kabuki Kebab (3/5)- Found it too dry. Taste wise as well, bland due to Chickpeas.
6. Chukunder ke Kebab (4/5)- Beetroot galauti, it was nice.
7. Kumbh ke Galawat (5/5)- Wow. Has to be the one thing I'd come back for! Surprisingly it was served on a thepla kind of bread.
8. Aloo Atishi (4/5)- Bharwan aloo, classic.

Mains :
1. Paneer Kaliyan (10/5)- Now that's what I'm talking about. It's dishes like these that make you remember these hotels. No one does mains like them. I ALWAYS skip mains but yesterday I was too tempted after a bite and ended up having 3 pieces of bread after such heavy array of starters. MY FAVOURITE FROM YESTERDAY because it was cashew gravy, so creamy, soft paneer chunks and rightly spiced. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED.
2. Daal Nawab (10/5)- Probably as good as the one I've had in Delhi at ITC. Properly slow cooked for 48 hours, perfectly creamy and texture was just right. Not overdone. Yum.
3. Subz Biryani - couldn't dare to try it. Was too full.

Desserts : Assorted variety
Motichoor Laddoo, Kaju Katli, Kalakand, Gulab Jamun, Akhroti Halwa, Phirni - all were great but my favourites were the Phirni and Gulab Jamun.

Great staff and great hospitality by team. marathe","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUssUCYhnHc-XMqbKZ_fRahgpNtdkY5Qe4BDdjBFhrI5sfKdUyhxw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nawab Saheb as the name suggests it’s a luxurious fine dine restaurant located in the basement of Renaissance Powai. This place was in my wish list since a very long time. I was amazed by the interiors and ambience of the place. The place can accommodate around 100 people at a time.

This place has maintained not just the standards but also taste of the food. The staff was very welcoming and warm with hospitality. Nawab Saheb is a gastronomical journey for those exploring the ageless Royal Mughlai and North Indian Cuisine.

The menu boasts of different menus such as Mix & Match, Seafood, Non-Vegetarian, Vegetarian and Jain options. Also, they have A-La-Carte menu. All these are eloquently curated by their Chef.

We started with Mix & Match Platter. The following dishes were adorned in the Platter.

Appetizers:

Nawabi Aloo Ki Nazakat

Chef Yunus Khan Ka Paneer

Dhuan Achari Lobster

Jheenga-e- Nisha

Akbari Murg Tikka

Shahi Gilawat Kebab

Mains:

Awadhi Paneer Korma

Makhamali Jheenga

Mahi Dum Qaliyan

Lagan Hara Murg

Potli Nihari

Dal Nawab Saheb

Gosht Awadhi Biryani

Desserts:

They have a dessert buffet in which you will get many Indian desserts. So, if you’re fan of Indian desserts it’s a perfect end to your meal.

Overall, a brilliant experience with the expertise in culinary world.

Food: 5/5

Ambience: 5/5

Décor: 5/5

Value for Money: 5/5

Ashar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW1OIKG1gVqkxdQ3bLJQigsywt3P94CqCbCKwL3cIL_vpnSoq4h=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nawab Sahab - Westin Powai
Amazing place , richness all over , menu curated by master Chef Yunus Khan a fusion of awadh , frontier India and Rajasthan
This place is known for it's Kebabs and mouth watering aromatic curries

Here is what we had :
==================

Veg and non veg platter - makai motiya seekh minced corn charred with Indian spices normally served on skewers. Since it was a platter it was a single piece
YONUS KHAN KA PANEER TIKKA - signature dish. Middle had a filling of dry fruits and nuts. Indian spices tandoor well with spices balanced
Subz gauloti - melt in mouth minced veggies with herbs and spices served over a bread. So Lucknow ( must try )
MALAI KA PHOOL - richness all over . Cheese and cream marinated broccoli charred.
Perfect

Non veg platter - Mahira babuli kebab famous dish of amritsar on bones. Melt in mouth tangy spicy marinating. Must try
GALAWAT KE KEBAB - do try this , served on a small paratha. Minced lamb with spices .balanced was prefect. Normally served on a griddle. Since it was a platter just had one piece which was enough to know the culinary talent of the master chef.

Samunder ki apsara - wow this was something new . Small lobster tandoor to perfection. Special spices used by the chef. I will remember this . Never had it as a starter

Coming to mains :
VEG - do try the Aminamadi  Saag , a spinach preparation with garlic and cumin. Freshly made full of flavours. Chef has kept it simple . Let the dish do the talking

Paneer Lazeez e khaas again a signature by chef. Chunks of cottage cheese with rich tomato gravy . Subtle in spices and aromatic at the same time.
However the 48 hour Long process Dal e yunus is worth a wait , rich urad lentil. Goes well with biryani or breads.

Non veg - ghost nihari tho banta hai boss . Bone marrow stock , lamb slowly cooked gives that aromatic taste and flavour.
Do try the murg handi lazeez - braised chicken with base of onion gravy along with methi ( fenugreek ) and cream. Chicken was cooked well tender and juicy

Must say that food , kebab or the gravy are not heavy or oily. Just perfect.
Missed out the prawns kasundi , lovely hit of kasundi cooked well. Velvety tomato and anar gravy. Do try.

Small portion of Subz Chilman biryani , long grain basmati rice with saffron a bit of Ittar ghee and veggies

Breads - Taftaan / Khameeri / Naan
However the dessert had a pleasant surprise
Had a capsicum halwa FOR the first time.
Something different which one needs to try out

Overall experience = awesome
Felt like a Nawab Dining at this place paired with a sparkling wine

Big thanks to Saurabh Dixit and team Westin for hosting

Ps. Only drawback , could not meet the master chef as had a day Off
Next time for sure
Till we meet again , Location & ambience
•	Gallops is located inside the Mahalaxmi Race Course in Mumbai, which gives it a uniquely lush, open-green backdrop uncommon in the city’s dining scene.  ￼
•	The décor and setting combine colonial charm with upscale comfort: for example, walnut-floors, candle-light chandeliers, and equestrian-themed décor post-revamp.  ￼
•	Outdoor and al-fresco dining is one of its strong suits — especially nice in cooler months.  ￼

2. Food quality & menu variety
•	The menu successfully blends Indian and Continental dishes. From traditional kebabs and curries to refined European-style mains, there’s something for varied tastes.  ￼
•	Signature dishes that get strong mentions:
•	“Dori Kebab” (mutton or chicken) – minced, skewered and charcoal-cooked.  ￼
•	“Chicken Ala Kiev” with cheese/butter-filled centre, as a refined European choice.  ￼
•	Desserts are also strong: Baked Alaska and newer additions like Baileys Caramel Cloud.  ￼
•	Many reviewers emphasize the generous portions, good presentation and a sense of value for a fine-dining venue.  ￼

3. Service & experience
•	The service is frequently praised: attentive staff, good recommendations (e.g., for wine or pairings), smooth reservation experience when booked in advance.  ￼
•	The overall dining experience is described as memorable— especially when everything aligns (good table, nice view, well-timed service). For example:
“What a wonderful restaurant from the minute we arrived the staff were so efficient and helpful, the food was absolutely incredible.”  ￼

4. Ideal for special occasions / date nights
•	The ambience, décor and location make it ideal for anniversaries, special dinners or when you want to treat yourself. Many mentions of formative visits, nostalgic moments, photo-friendly setting.  ￼

If I were picking one restaurant for a refined Mumbai meal — especially if you’re after something more than just dinner but an experience — Gallops checks a lot of boxes:
•	A setting that feels premium yet relaxed.
•	A menu that offers both familiar Indian comfort and elevated Continental touches.
•	Good enough service to let you focus on the evening rather than logistics.
•	A chance to dine in one of Mumbai’s less-hustle zones (given the race-course setting) while still being accessible.

If you ask me: go for the outdoor or window-view table (book ahead), order one of the signature starters + a main you haven’t tried, and finish with one of the classic desserts. [{"Name":"Rachit Chandra","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXMcLQJewn5TUn33tcr9iFWYKEaa81rV6-mMI4R5d0Au3ZH20a2Mg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Leopold Cafe is one of Mumbai’s most iconic and historic cafés, known not just for its food but also for its deep connection with the city’s past. One of the most striking and emotional aspects of the place is that the gunshot marks from the 26/11 terror attack are still preserved, making it a powerful reminder of history and resilience.

The food and drinks are good in taste and reasonably priced compared to the location and popularity of the café. They offer a wide variety of options, including Indian, Continental, Chinese food, snacks, and beverages, making it suitable for all kinds of visitors. Portions are decent, and service is fairly quick considering the crowd.

The café is extremely crowded on weekends, especially in the evenings, so you may have to wait for a table. The atmosphere is lively, vibrant, and full of energy, attracting tourists, locals, and international visitors alike. The staff manages the crowd well and remains polite despite the rush.

Located in Colaba, it’s very close to major attractions like Gateway of India and is easy to reach. Overall, Leopold Cafe is a must-visit place in Mumbai for its history, legacy, lively vibe, and value-for-money food. A place you should experience at least The atmosphere here is so Lively and happy always, but very noisy because of the open space downstairs. Upstairs is quiet.

There was a waiting time to get in. We stood in the queue for 15-20 mins. On reaching the head of the queue, we realised there were 2 queues on opposite sides of the main entrance. They definitely need better process/management of the queue. Since the lower floor was full, we sat at the bar counter on the first floor. There are better cafes and restaurants in Colaba.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1jrSUsk4j2KeCXeDKMMwpLFDIt7E-Gj_iAzdhG77Rnuch5CWPktfYRVdZliy2mytfNmJTFhS5v5tCcs7MgtNnP9dupxH61q1VD43KGs4e1dA8dbFKZy2VwrkPZtQx_FptXMS3qp1J0BFA=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2cq61oN2l2r1LUZ751ABDhJT8CnhcmPgtO9ixFzJ_v0VEvcZHaB2wFluo8BVippi3w3V59Tz-XNNYIA0Ts2TQsiNYTNfYlz4FRyq9fockExm6DtUnQpEcTf7SHPO-imHu5VXV3I3ANoVN2=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3mTG4YDRotUWdflYASQL4oI1N8XTDVKBiO35EJ9XIq_9BY7aKwabAf9_IIfH1tebGb7s-Zyrh63ymIQkCAyKLwHaT62PQ9u8BZQzvJBMYEnj6490I9S0iGsPJrcm2OyrX8I6589JXiF6SX=k-no"],"When":"2026-2-22"},{"Name":"Abhijeet Thigale","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXcKr5KdRDrQ_0xVgJrcu-THILOCoTZKSU-MspvZHEIdOorji_RrQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Mumbai's Iconic Hangout Spot
Leopold Cafe in Colaba, Mumbai, is more than just a restaurant—it's a living piece of the city's history. Established in 1871, this Irani-style cafe gained global fame (and tragedy) as one of the sites targeted during the 2008 Mumbai attacks. The bullet holes in the walls are still preserved as a somber reminder, adding a layer of historical weight to the place.
The vibe is unbeatable: retro, bustling, and full of character. Ceiling fans whirl overhead, walls are plastered with old posters and photos, and the place hums with energy from tourists, locals, and backpackers. It's perfect for people-watching, especially if you snag a table outside on the pavement or on the upper deck when available. The atmosphere alone makes it feel like stepping into a different era of Mumbai.
Food-wise, the menu is massive—covering Irani classics, continental, Chinese, and more. Portions are generous, and some dishes stand out, like the Chinese options (beef teriyaki and chilli items get frequent praise), Shepherd's Pie, or walnut pie for dessert. The draught beer is a hit too. That said, the food is solid but not exceptional—it's more about the experience than gourmet dining. Expect hearty cafe fare rather than fine cuisine.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1tIuteKWbylbfveaErrDR8CYELWW4BKDpvkIFV8LLCtr0CL_1xChSm6-VE1iUBwzPY234ARjOmqBedNVHwS6SOQA8RC9ySfULwq3-dv0_l8YV6VmcJkqG93PGKQ1L_S0c9nBO02FAxqhRp=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2mM8aW8s8wJtYgVMUm6wAR1J9Ss1HKaZw_06iieaPvt463eNEXr83EAuhE3WehzteWpAHbqWMmRS8ozCdfiLeU2tZ_QzLN4TeWbBKitjfKhEZRiZRVpMfaQQXzS6EN1XRtGTR4THjD1sEj=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1G5fZ1OuAbN76fvchnGxZt4Az0RKpiJy_AlQg7B_T4KNrZUOKVJtr_JrZamTgh2KtHKc6ssULSg0U-YbtrqoD-PEYeteJ5mgGAX52HlGPU7aExu2hJTdjvPuHw5a7-wJorR0Q_B7EaSMV0=k-no"],"When":"2026-1-27"},{"Name":"Nicole Cherian Furtado","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUpE5Ns980oR69d5HoXQOcLA4cPLfkd3ap2qwVOwCxE8k3MMOwKJg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Leopold Cafe was amazing.
