[{"Name":"Saqlain Sethia","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXRoWqoidvkJyw-9rNiz0AcxiasZcqbnC2hm_x16bDU-LoZUriZ3w=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you’re a true food lover, Noor Mohammadi Hotel is nothing short of heaven. I finally got to try their legendary Nalli Nihari, and honestly… it lives up to every bit of the hype.

The nihari was rich, slow-cooked to perfection, with the marrow melting into the gravy, bursting with deep, soulful flavors. Every spoon felt warm, comforting, and indulgent — the kind of dish that makes you pause and appreciate good food.

And just when I thought it couldn’t get better, the meal ended with their Rabdi. Thick, creamy, and perfectly balanced in sweetness, it was the ideal dessert to round off such a hearty feast. You can actually taste the patience and tradition in every bite.
No fancy plating, no unnecessary drama — just authentic, old-school flavors that hit straight to the heart. Noor Mohammadi doesn’t just serve food; it serves heritage on a plate.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1ZPJWQdanK_4NLQfJF2xoTAnlcL6mGQ_GCTWDx9SrJAD4BnFYeWARp96uSvwndBz-jgQVKxyd27lY7BjdHfWwIKgFZnLIt21LL4KXXFmlVigCLHXSWwjbrSgptocwoMmRgABbGYeORfCnX=k-no"],"When":"2026-1-27"},{"Name":"VeeEssKay","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/local-reviews/AJMZ0QewvQXK08PhtQgCu1fT7TcdWcgcaXdOmFk48xW5xlmLo-Eqy0UOp2YqveGvl0etYz5BzA=s120-c-br100","Rating":0,"Description":"We visited this restaurant on a Saturday afternoon with the main intention of trying their Nalli Nihari, but to our disappointment, we were told that Nalli Nihari finishes fast on Saturdays and Sundays (around 10 am)||||We were then left with very few options as the menu of this restaurant is very limited. We ordered two pieces of Shami Kebab (it’s sold by piece), only one was served. We had to wait for the second piece for quite a long, and in spite of the long wait, what we got is a doughy textured kebab which was not as good as the first one. A usual excuse was offered that it is fresh from the tawa hence the texture. Though the taste was good, I did not like the texture much. I think this fast moving item here is little overhyped.||||We then ordered Mutton Paya and Chicken Sanjoo Baba (the so called recipe of Sanjay Dutt). Both the dishes were distinct in taste and satisfying. Chicken Sanju baba was little sweet, but the spices (khada masala) used in this dish were very tasty. It makes me think that this dish was cooked in ghee because after 10 minutes, the gravy got frozen and turned itself into lumps. So finish it fast as soon as it us served  (and try to sit on the ground floor non AC section to avoid this)||||The tandoori rotis served were very excellent and tasty too (also large in size)||||As the portion size was little small for the main course, we ordered one more Mutton Chop (or Chaap?). I didn’t know that this item would come with gravy. This dish was so awesome and nicely spiced that it satisfied all our taste buds.||||The finale was done with caramel custard and rabdi. Both the dessert items were exceptionally good (but again small in portion size). The rabdi was served in earthern pot (matka) which enhanced the taste and experience ||||The total cost of this treat was Rs. 650 (for two persons) which was damn cheap by any standards. ||||The ambiance is not so good, though we sat in the Air-conditioned section on the mezzanine floor. In fact, it gave us a stinking smell of drainage as soon as we entered. As pointed out by some other reviewer, this hotel should pay more attention to the hygiene and cleanliness (even it comes at a little extra cost)||||The rating of 4/5 has been given only on the basis of the taste of the food","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Sk Md Sohail Amin","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXcfdeEyVGNsMK8K5L28gHoJk2hicwnUMVeZWNjuOo1kzU4HAbbmA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Noor Mohammadi Hotel on Mohammad Ali Road is one of Mumbai’s old-school Mughlai legends, known for its rich, no-nonsense non-vegetarian fare and deep-rooted history.

The place is modest and often crowded, but the food does all the talking—especially the iconic Nalli Nihari, the much-loved Chicken Sanju Baba, flavourful Kebabs, and comforting Dal Ghee with rotis. Dont know about the taste, but hygiene and health is more important which I assure is worst at this place...","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Shaikh Kadir","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWQAmwnZ3vNi1moz-p72byY-tYGgYHltDoj3Pg9dK0BPymGpxTNDw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This place truly gave me a heartwarming experience and the kind of food that reminds you what real, honest cooking tastes like. It’s amazing to know that this restaurant has been around for over 102 years, carrying forward a legacy of authentic flavors and consistent quality. You can really feel that heritage in every dish they serve.

The food was absolutely delicious and clearly made with care and good-quality ingredients. I loved how every bite had that traditional touch — nothing fancy, just pure, flavorful food made right. My personal favorites were the Mutton Sanju Baba and the Haleem. The mutton was soft, juicy, and full of flavor, while the haleem was rich, creamy, and perfectly spiced — the kind that instantly makes you smile after the first spoonful.

The ambiance adds to the experience — simple, calm, and full of character. It feels like a place where stories have been shared for generations. The staff was really friendly and attentive, making sure we were comfortable and enjoying our food.

Overall, I’d say this isn’t just a restaurant it’s a part of culinary history. KOKO in Lower Parel offers a refined and memorable dining experience. The ambiance is top-notch — modern, elegant, and perfect for an evening out. The menu features an impressive variety of sushi and dim sum, with ample vegetarian choices that are just as flavorful and thoughtfully prepared as the non-vegetarian options.

The staff is courteous and attentive, adding to the overall premium feel of the place. However, a heads-up for first-timers — they don’t usually offer tables without a reservation, even if some appear to be vacant. Be prepared to sit at the bar if you walk in without prior booking.

That said, once you’re seated, the food and experience are absolutely worth it. Great location, super friendly staff and a great choice of high quality food for all diet restrictions. They even have special gluten free dishes. The first restaurant which even offers gluten-free soy sauce. Fantastic so even I could enjoy the wonderful flavour of soy sauce and miso soup.
You can ask the team for some extras like you want dishes without onions or garlic...
All the dishes were flavoured perfectly and with love.

The dessert was a creamy fluffy dream. And the espresso too.
Absolutely all on point.

[{"Name":"Harshad Dixit","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVWMl8O_tzwdPx7vftoZy3-neYo3-6Hr6b-KXncS87Bbhevo90CPA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"We got the Christmas tasting menu, some cocktails and some of us got the wine pairing. Overall a great experience considering the price we paid.

A tasting menu for the Indian palate should mean you say 'wow' or atleast 'nice' after every course and Ekaa delivered. 5/7 courses were awesome, the fired chicken was a tad salty for me and the last dessert course was just ok. We were too stuffed to appreciate it maybe. Vegetarian food was hit and miss, the carrots we had were underwhelming.

In any case, highly recommend trying it if you are in Mumbai. Interestingly they have an ala carte menu as well. Service is very good. Table assignment can improve. Dont tell customers a table is reserved if not true.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Abhishek Pandey","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXcycunM1-Hlno1CZdHd3HWpWOGCOjusHMWKeKLAlXPWxmpXkdB9g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"Food Quality and Authenticity:
The restaurant's signature dish, the "Jamavar Dal," falls dramatically short of expectations. Despite its premium pricing and marketed prestige, it appears to be a basic dal tarka masquerading as something more sophisticated. For connoisseurs of Indian cuisine, the distinction is immediately apparent - this is not the rich, complex lentil preparation one would expect from a high-end establishment.

Service Efficiency:
The kitchen's pace is frustratingly glacial. Even basic items like rotis, which are fundamental to Indian dining and typically prepared quickly in most establishments, seem to get lost in an inexplicable time void. This slow service significantly impacts the dining experience and disrupts the natural flow of the meal.

Value for Money:
The pricing structure appears to be more aligned with the restaurant's ambitions than its actual delivery. [{"Name":"Kiran Pathade","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXI0Pp0hTiPq1Z3Kv17JBTUc0my6a341SK5-BucLjGTvBnCHCQ2=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"Had been to Indian Accent, Mumbai today for lunch. Our meal started with the Blue Cheese Naan and Indian Accent Shorba. I really liked the shorba — it was creamy and had a very balanced taste. The blue cheese naan, however, was not to my liking as the flavour was quite overpowering.
We had also ordered Raspberry Soda and Cherry Cola, but both didn’t appeal to my taste.
We then ordered the Pulled Jackfruit Phulka Taco, which was one of the highlights. It had a subtle pickled taste that made it unique and very enjoyable. The Anar and Churan Kulfi served as a palate cleanser was refreshing and a nice touch.
For mains, we had Laccha Paratha, Chur Chur Naan, 100 Layer Paneer, Tamatar Chaman and Nadru, and Dal Moradabadi. The Dal Moradabadi stood out with its fried dal texture and slight tanginess, offering a flavour profile different from dals served elsewhere.
We finished with Daulat ki Chaat, which was excellent. The dessert was light, delicate, and not overly sweet, just perfect.
Service was excellent. The server explained each dish in detail and was very attentive throughout, ensuring a seamless dining experience.
The highlights of the meal were the Jackfruit Taco, Dal Moradabadi, and Daulat ki Chaat. Overall, it was a well-curated and memorable dining experience.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn33aCG2s8K764A2tYGw77scXvq_N1CMhAgQdAYUB5G2IHruI5MfdFiTCRW91zxV4j53IBaZrMw9aNetxtJfXm-ERtHhbYLlGM6_7Bheu5plWFIowOrbHFwR49WNnJQPlyWX8xsxuDbMyN6O=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0FzjNCV3SDqdXlJjHDLt3x7ny5zVBZrs1J-CSoDm-mpGzKUCQnISarnVLScnydM6bY5c7-EjOciJTvCOxt5P1Up_qoL9nyRPsYDD9ks430Y0j3kXRGn0kN9exy-V2oAkxxsZAZE_j_SSr0=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2qJuIw2WnNsnwIkkrFwXnTb4dEtJ5QTDlRPM8mqn9LKY3R9-cM2P6WyQOg1cvzcrS9Jd0PZ-b3X56umBbKQSBvcNaFiRapp9PMqIQ_prFZ1BpNuumMKJSv_iSs0vcD8aTTy7EJWn8r_l0a=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0fmPU9im0ZJpkTwHNEi6oWiwfAlrAR28wZQqzO3N5pHy2z19b_mC0czO9M061NgLHwlsgDJVWf2Fgw03SW5dxQZ7vVFcO0x-nogXsnijwd6ljE5OkssKGYU4hUiheVc59gOFH1gm7AWHTj=k-no"],"When":"2026-2-15"},{"Name":"Sneha N","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKpf0VMiSi8u1gUe9swNQyxLFrIotMkOZrb8lz2CQUObMXxaw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing dinner start to finish. experience!

We had an absolutely wonderful evening at Indian Accent. The food was delectable — so good that we ended up ordering almost the entire à la carte menu! Every dish was thoughtfully crafted, flavourful, and had that signature modern twist the restaurant is known for. We were literally eating with our hands because it was that delicious.

A special mention to Chef Rijul Gulati, who guided us beautifully through the seasonal offerings. Since it’s winter, he recommended the saag and a few other seasonal vegetables — each one cooked to perfection and presented with a contemporary flair. It truly felt like Indian cuisine elevated with the most innovative and modern touch.

The highlight of the night, however, was the makhan malai and the daulat ki chaat. Being from the North, and having grown up craving these winter delicacies, this was pure nostalgia. The makhan malai was the softest cloud — airy, delicate, and absolutely divine. The perfect end to an already exceptional meal.

Everything we ate was wonderful, and the experience from start to finish was memorable.
Highly I visited Indian Accent for my birthday on Jan 10th. While the presentation of the food is undoubtedly beautiful, the overall experience didn't live up to the reputation or the price tag. Despite having a reservation and seeing many empty tables, we were made to wait outside for 15 minutes.
The food was "fine," but nothing was particularly memorable. We tried the pulled jackfruit tacos, chicken tikka meatballs, the hundred layer paneer and a few other items, but none stood out in terms of flavor. The Daulat Ki Chaat was just okay, and while the complimentary roasted almond dessert was a nice gesture, it was quite underwhelming. The drinks (G&T, Campari, and Caol Ila) were significantly overpriced. For the total cost, you could enjoy a much better meal at two other high-end restaurants. [{"Name":"Gayatri","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWrFtPy54soQLCyo5RDXrv3CDDL_girByahWGALPpZefBqMLs0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had a wonderful lunch at Rasoi and everything was simply delightful!
• Firdausi Aloo – Perfectly spiced, rich and flavourful with a royal touch.
• Karari Rumali Roti – Crisp, light and beautifully done with the right crunch.
• Corn & Water Chestnut Starters – Fresh, crunchy and a great balance of sweetness and spice.
• Aromatic Green Thai Curry – Fragrant, creamy and packed with authentic flavours.
• Zafrani Rasmalai – Soft, melt-in-the-mouth and infused with delicate saffron notes.
• Anar Ka Pyaar (Mocktail) – Refreshing, fruity and perfectly balanced.
• Thanda Teekha (Mocktail) – A fun blend of sweet and spicy that really stood out.
The service was quick and seamless, and the staff was extremely knowledgeable about the menu, offering great recommendations. Since we visited on Holi, they surprised us with complimentary Jalebi Rabdi and Thandai, which made the celebration even more special.
A truly memorable dining experience — highly recommended! Quadras","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUxrbOwvh9QjgjxYnkg7o9N2ubjmz5qIV3zmPTf0xE9EH0k2ANG=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"If you find yourself navigating the busy lanes of Dadar East, there’s a culinary refuge just a few minutes’ walk from the station. Located within the familiar premises of Ramee Hotels, Rasoi Kitchen and Bar offers a surprisingly diverse menu that bridges the gap between soulful North Indian staples and zesty Oriental favorites.

We kicked things off with a round of mocktails. While the Tamarind Ginger Sour—a refreshing blend of ginger ale and tangy tamarind—was a cooling palate cleanser, the real star was Anar ka Pyar. This pomegranate-based drink was the undisputed winner of the night; it was vibrant, balanced, and high-quality through and through.

For appetizers, we leaned into their Oriental selection. The Kung Pao Chicken is a crowd favorite here for a reason—it hits all the right notes of spice and crunch. We also sampled the Chicken Enoki Dimsums. For anyone craving something delicate yet flavorful, these are a great choice; they aren't spicy, allowing the subtle flavors of the Enoki to really shine.

The highlight of the main course was undoubtedly the Nawabi Murgh. This Lucknowi-style chicken tikka gravy was rich, thick, and wonderfully creamy. The meat remained juicy, making it the perfect partner for a hot, crisp Butter Naan. To round off the meal, we opted for the Zafrani Rasmalai. It was a decent, saffron-infused end to a very satisfying dinner.

Beyond the food, the vibe at Rasoi is amplified by their live music sessions, which really elevate the dining experience from a simple meal to a full evening of entertainment. Whether you’re a local or just passing through Dadar, Rasoi’s blend of exceptional food and lively ambiance makes it a stop worth Chikhalkar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUziTONgXIM2RfN1ZlMnNFJU-J4vDTyN4guDKwGJ34CqUdT_MzzLw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"I recently visited Rasoi Kitchen by Ramee Hospitality in Dadar, and it was a fantastic culinary experience! The restaurant has a lovely royal decor and a warm, inviting atmosphere that makes it perfect for a relaxed meal.
The food was absolutely delicious. We started with the Rasoi Paneer Tikka, which was perfectly charred and flavorful, and the Mushroom in Hakka Style was a great fusion surprise. For the main course, the Paneer Neebu Do Pyaza was a standout—creamy with a refreshing citrusy tang that paired beautifully with the fresh Roti Basket and Steamed Rice.
The drinks were a highlight as well; the Tamarind Ginger Sour and Thanda Teekha provided a unique, spicy kick that you don't find everywhere. We ended on a high note with their in-house Kulfi Falooda, which was rich, authentic, and the perfect dessert.

Here’s what we tried:
Mocktails:
Fruit Sangria and Thanda Teekha – Both drinks were excellent and a perfect way to start the meal. They were unique, refreshing, and had a great balance of flavors.
Starters:
Bhatti Da Murgh – The chicken legs were very well marinated and packed with rich, smoky flavors.
Balinese BBQ Chicken – Perfectly cooked chicken with spot-on flavors that were truly satisfying.
Main Course:
Paneer Neebu Do Pyaza – Absolutely mind-blowing! The gravy was so perfectly balanced that I completely fell in love with it. The portion size was generous, with a good quantity of paneer, and both quality and taste were outstanding. Highly recommended!
Butter Naan – Soft, fluffy, and nicely buttery.
Chicken Fried Rice – Delicious and fulfilling, loaded with chicken pieces and egg.
Overall, a fantastic dining experience with great food, quality portions, and excellent service. Fresh green chutney, sliced onions, mirchi, and pickle on the side instantly elevate the flavours, adding that extra punch to every bite.
The Sigri Murg is smoky, juicy, and full of depth, while the Tamarind Tandoori Chicken Wings strike the perfect balance between tangy and spicy. The Nawabi Murg is rich, creamy, and indulgent, best enjoyed with soft, buttery Garlic Naan. The Pad Thai Noodles bring a well-balanced fusion of sweet, savoury, and nutty notes. But it can be ignored.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kshama Patel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVC-Jo53nF8lZG0yfSZWveWt_r98EmdlUKILzANe0sz5ZwObWc=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Dining at Peshawri in ITC Maratha was nothing short of nostalgic. Their legendary Dal Bukhara tasted exactly the same as I remember having it 14 years ago in India rich, slow-cooked, deeply comforting, and utterly unforgettable. That kind of consistency over decades is no accident; it’s a true testament to their culinary discipline and mastery.

What made the experience even more special was embracing tradition eating with our hands, just the way this food is meant to be enjoyed. The thoughtful details stood out: being handed an apron without hesitation, and later a bowl of hot water with lemon to cleanse and scrub a small but powerful reminder that Peshawri honors not just flavor, but ritual.

Every bite felt grounded in heritage, patience, and pride.

A heartfelt thank you to our wonderful table hosts Cleatus, Anish, Nano, and Prashma for making the evening seamless and memorable. Hospitality like this elevates great food into an unforgettable experience.

Some tastes don’t change — and thankfully, this one never should. 🍛✨","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Smitesh Wadher","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXNAMWJy5M7ASNpEmAx8t88eI11hJY_wxIgJfM7uRg9WaBkkYLiDA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Amazing Culinary Experience at Peshawri - ITC Maratha

I recently visited Peshawri and was blown away by the exceptional food and service. The variety and flavors in their dishes are outstanding. The paneer and tandoori vegetables were spiced to perfection, while the rolls were crisp on the outside and soft on the inside.

Special mention goes to their Daal Bukhara—it was creamy, rich, and one of the best I’ve ever tasted. Every dish was a perfect blend of traditional spices and expert preparation.

The presentation was also great, and the fresh accompaniments like sliced onions, cucumbers, and tomatoes added a nice touch to the plates.

Highly recommended if you’re looking for authentic, flavorful Indian cuisine. I will definitely be coming On a rainy evening I decided to rekindle my memories of a  memorable Bukhara meal at the ITC Maurya, Delhi over a decade back.

Luckily I got a table and quickly chose the single diner menu which is the best for a solo diner like me. The service is impeccable, personalised and attentive without being intrusive.

The ‘daal makhani/kaali daal’ is the star of the meal, absolutely lip smacking to be relished with a soft paratha or roti. A spoon of the daal by itself is also a treat to the taste buds.

The kebabs were well done, the seekh kebab, malai chicken tikka and the mutton with a dash of lime and the steaming hot roti or paratha with daal is sheer ambrosia. The butter chicken was good but sadly I couldn’t even finish half of it.

To end the meal, the malai kulfi does the trick. It assuages the spice burst of the previous course.

Drinks & Food: We tried their mocktails, which were super refreshing, especially the watermelon-based one. The bruschetta was also tasty and had a nice balance of flavors.
Ambience: The place has a very cozy and relaxed vibe, perfect for hanging out with friends late into the night.

Tucked away inside a small lane, it’s a little difficult to spot. After the madness of the fest, this café felt like a lake in a desert, felt calm, comforting, and exactly what we needed.
The vibe is warm and lively at the same time, with a charming setup and even a hidden bar counter that adds to the character of the place.
Coming to the food and drinks, they are the absolute winners. I tried the Masala Cappuccino, and it was truly unique, with a lovely kick of masala chai flavours blended into the coffee. Something I’ve never had before and surprisingly addictive.
The Cheese Chilli Toast, recommended by the waiter, did not disappoint—super cheesy with perfectly crispy bread. The Cottage Cheese Burger was honestly to die for, easily one of the best vegetarian burgers I’ve had—flavourful, filling, and perfectly balanced.
And the real star of the show was the Hash Brown dish “Rosti, crispy on the outside, creamy on the inside, and just cooked to perfection. Absolute comfort food.
Overall, great food, lovely coffee, welcoming vibes, and a peaceful break from the festival rush. The café has that cozy, heritage charm and friendly service that everyone raves about.

Wife had the Ham and Cheese Classic Sandwich, I had the Mutton Sandwich, and our son went for the Chicken Schnitzel. We shared a Rocket, Fruit & Honey Salad, the Ginger Cake with cream, and a bowl of Roast Potatoes.

Everything was fresh and nicely done — except the mutton sandwich, which, in my humble opinion, should have been the classic kind: just butter, salt, pepper, and a touch of whole grain mustard between two soft slices of bread. Instead, this version tried a little too hard to impress and somehow lost its soul in the process.

The place was very crowded and cloudy (from the shisha), which created a lively, buzzing atmosphere. They do offer shisha, and the drinks were made really well — definitely one of the highlights of the night.

The staff were polite, attentive, and helpful, which always makes a big difference, especially on such a busy evening. Service stayed smooth despite the crowd, which I appreciated.

The only downside was the music — it was mostly older songs, and the vibe could have been elevated with a better DJ and a more upbeat playlist for a Valentine’s crowd. The crowd itself was decent, nothing too wild, just a mixed and social scene.

Me and my group of friends had paid around 18k at the time of booking itself. We had to wait outside for a while since they were having an argument with some other customers. Then upon entering, they said we will not be able to provide you a table, so we'll have to stand at the bar. However all the tables in the restaurant were empty, they had forced everyone there to stand at the bar, saying the tables can't be given to us unless we pay an additional 5k per head. After a lot of argument they finally relented and gave us a table with 4 seats when we were a group of 7, but only until 11:00, after which they would start charging us the 5k.
We ordered some starters and within 2 mins the waiter arrived with the bill, saying he will keep bringing the bill after every order since it's very busy, so we will have to settle up everytime right then and there, including the service charge which they had very conveniently made mandatory for the night.
When we had entered, they had given us multiple coupons of 1k each, totaling 18k, to use to settle up the tab. At one point, when the bill had reached 13098, we paid them 13k in coupons and asked them to carry forward the 98 for the next bill. The waiter however kept demanding that we should give him 1 more coupon of 1000, which also he would not adjust in the next bill. So basically he wanted us to pay 1000 for a mere additional 98. All of this "chhindigiri" is happening when we have already paid 18k, so either way the restaurant was as it is in a profit of 5k.
This level of cheapness is the last thing any of us expected from a restaurant that claims to be so posh and high class. The waiter and manager were very rude, we had to physically go to the kitchen to ask for water multiple times since they refused to actually pay attention to the order we were asking them to bring.
Horrible service from a place that charges exorbitant rates from people in exchange for a pleasant experience.
I understand the concept of supply and demand, but they are simply taking advantage of people, especially on a popular night and robbing their customers blind.
Never will any of my family, friends, or their family and friends ever go to this disgusting establishment again.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Eva","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJEFc_ejyfniFM2LB8RRgAszJso2tmD0gAbt22nKu26reVxG1k=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Really good food and drinks! This was our first visit to your establishment, and the experience was deeply disappointing. Ambience: very calm, tranquil ambience. The window offers a beautiful view of the sea and the queens necklace. The atmosphere is very pleasant.

Food : superb food in all aspects whether it's modern - fusion food or good ol classic butter chicken! Would recommend the pumpkin barley soup, the smoked dal makhani, butter chicken, and palak samosa.

Service: best in class service. Ms. Maria was very helpful from the beginning in prompt response to the booking and helping us out with the set menu options.
Mr. Siddharth and Ms. Chari were top notch in their service. Paying attention to all our needs they went above and beyond to make sure we had everything we wanted.

Value for money: The portion size is relatively smaller compared to the pricing, but the main course is quite sufficient. The overall experience was definitely worth paying for.

Pro tips :
1. go at sharp 7.00 pm when the restaurant opens if you want the best table with the view as these are limited.
2. We had a party of 5, so opted for the set menu option which allows you to plan in advance exactly what the pricing is going to be like. They have 3 different types of set menus along with Al carte.
3. Definitely recommend booking a table in advance.

[{"Name":"Akshatha Maindan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX_rYb9LDBqNETFyYrHQf1iwetddQ7-VghDl8zQTKPgJsCn_CwPJw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"This place had been on our wishlist for quite some time, and we finally visited for a birthday lunch. It definitely lived up to our expectations.

The food instantly reminded us of Goa. The prawn and squid dishes were prepared really well — fresh, flavorful, and slightly on the spicier side with a noticeable pepper kick, which we enjoyed. The poi (poe) was a standout, served fresh and perfectly complementing the curries. The mutton curry was absolutely delicious and one of the highlights of the meal.

The staff was courteous and helpful, guiding us with our order and making the experience smooth.

The place itself is done up beautifully and truly gives off a relaxed Goa vibe.

On our most recent visit — a Sunday — we were somewhat surprised to find ourselves waiting for nearly 15 minutes before being seated, despite having a paid and guaranteed reservation. While the restaurant’s popularity, especially over the weekend, is understandable, such a delay does take away slightly from the otherwise premium dining experience one expects with a confirmed booking. In contrast, our weekday visit was handled seamlessly, with immediate seating and a noticeably calmer ambience.

The service on both occasions, however, was exceptional. Every member of the staff was attentive, courteous, and eager to make the experience memorable. A special mention must go to David, who stood out for his warmth and proactive attitude. He went above and beyond to accommodate our preferences, even helping customize certain dishes — a gesture that reflected genuine hospitality and care.

Coming to the food, O Pedro continues to impress with its bold, vibrant flavors. The menu has a unique character — there’s a slight get-used-to element initially, as the dishes often play with contrasting and layered flavor profiles. Overall, the palate leans towards the spicier side, but once accustomed, it becomes quite addictive. Highly recommend trying the Jackfruit Pilaf, the Chorizo Fried Rice (adding an extra egg takes it up a notch), and the Butter Garlic Prawns, all of which stood out for their balance of flavor and quality of preparation.

The ambience, much like the experience, varies by day. On weekends, the place is packed with energy — the crowd is vibrant, the music is loud, and the overall buzz is palpable. While that lends a lively atmosphere, it can get slightly overwhelming when you’re looking for a quieter dining experience. On weekdays, however, O Pedro strikes the perfect note — with softer music, balanced lighting, and an ambience that lets you truly unwind and savor your meal.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1bRH5oICRD_-3ja5Uf5dfvyxpyos-i5CbQDJIghqQdqurlIEt0638HjvruDQNabj-lfOH02V_8_GBS_jQgs-Ipjje5fCk4KWwaM9_4kylIa_vL3Zh_cBoBEL7ViAt5CH-U-gZ1uFfKArY=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2aSza6Cg5Zi6Or64sg_-MOaftmM4d2tCtvwkGcGmSjNN6ogb00VEVRuKSn7GnPnxQ21GBa6AqJLurADyjeMy6meo0xXZDkb847aUpAdL-gyh7H0xKf4TL7rJSrpU6Ws4fMVg7ZLpwLZtA=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2BMj_T86iO9CXz_ACxm7ulb4KF2lYTCgAYCYou5YjmQq0CMvwbJrzeBf9xiNy122prp5cf01jz_P945l2NmHOZ_-NHiPos_CfBME6Wl2yXkPgAtZcguFwkViknf-wvVOuIUQZNkKrPBQ0=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0i55VlcxznetRKyXqHSmbCX-F6l_QHg76fVq5SZeYw1Fzbq0ORQugdZqEqrxUzB2_DthXinSpzI2JbshkBwx_2dOdosT4zNvG-ugsyvWpEHzUql5Q0eRKwt8Cc8RCbAmX4DUn7slkPq9EV=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3tNT0xR6C-JkwsqLbsM1cK6c3WgQfu04rnGND4DV0S55hHmAvEIZmIr_z0u5UJnboO1VLLfCwYj7hbfkQRWyQpKLIL_o73iTJvbTX0_wP-HUAw17YgAD8thfOiiXBOR7RSVyw9wBnQMzE=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2lFW2HsJzb0mOJ8SRMl6UYj1vc2oumBvPijTghTd9XDQ_gjB_siHfoWFyiTptFPi7RdiK7Xo4zaN8cogXsUVxYN8Wt7B6wV8rY-jf9rxo0_zZk1oQlzvT5Rbwcc7veBw1q4oPyKn5iEBY=k-no"],"When":"2025-10-6"}]

As a self-proclaimed foodie, I had heard rave reviews about this place and decided to give it a try. I can confidently say that Central Restaurant surpassed my expectations with their mouthwatering offerings.

Upon entering Central Restaurant, I was greeted by a cozy and inviting ambiance. The staff was warm and friendly, providing excellent service throughout my dining experience. The menu was extensive, but I was particularly interested in their kebab platters and tea selection.

The kebab platters at Central Restaurant were simply outstanding. The meat was tender, succulent, and bursting with flavor. Whether it was the juicy chicken tikka, the perfectly marinated seekh kebabs, or the melt-in-your-mouth lamb kebabs, each bite was a delight to the senses. The platters were served with a generous portion of aromatic rice, fresh salad, and delicious homemade naan bread, completing the culinary journey.

What impressed me further was their tea selection. Central Restaurant takes great pride in their tea offerings, and it shows. Their tea menu featured an array of options, including traditional Indian chai, herbal infusions, and exotic blends. I opted for their masala chai, and it was a revelation. The tea was brewed to perfection, striking the right balance between the robustness of spices and the soothing warmth of milk. It was the ideal accompaniment to the rich and flavorful kebabs.

The attention to detail in Central Restaurant's food preparation and presentation was commendable. Each dish was beautifully plated, showcasing the chef's expertise and passion for their craft. The flavors were authentic, and the quality of ingredients was evident in every bite.

Moreover, the pricing at Central Restaurant was reasonable, considering the generous portions and the exceptional taste. It is a place where you can enjoy a satisfying meal without breaking the bank

In conclusion, Central Restaurant at Kalbadevi is a haven for kebab and tea enthusiasts. With their delectable kebab platters and impressive tea selection, it is a place that delivers on both taste and ambiance. Whether you're a local or a visitor exploring the culinary scene of Mumbai, I highly recommend paying a visit to Central Restaurant. Prepare to indulge in a gastronomic experience that will leave you craving for more.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0Ru2ZVFk3rIZFI1ZbdkZN3Bc_AH6Y5MXyH0F1sHGvjiQBDifL7Q8ZJzgCPBChFsvAgjqJx-ulYkS3slQkncCTkUzOBmHn5-EEjDhbp3MBYDHQGLecnQNkQFtKk8RZE_z2lBisJdw=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3TOz_Mp24YR5jsLOd4seOGUGQeRDRHux3AfcV34UN9j8unYjScmiPxIKPaFG4GSCOQ0yEK6K-hlJfsCyVZ-QeMapmEXmqt7uX9DpqVGmM_pxZC9wcLq9C-LazJSOgUBDgx8CY=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2iyiGjQACt_aJ5l6akScVXLRWlP6Bw8m4_iH5oJCCb4NLjMSJNzN75cG1UJb5dCkuIJv1uqAiydDo_PumPN2ArUvGZoqC_jXH8Z60V8M4XNiEd-mgH51358OER4w8W4vfXI6AXAg=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3CX0F9UkETiPuGvQGc3P4p_m87W3TsCwuRgeMnVIHYmH5G8-koNx4ETca3J7cW4comQj0HFCQbIOjPcOIMdKy8oPa_s_Oys1865a6g-yBi4AwC7uj1uGS4zc7zE0fbT0UnU0Y=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn09vFuQ8Vhh_lFaeLZql0lGbbAV1AliX4tmYYbni9YKnLwCxW9q9Y127XBMmKcuVgn3JjTu7goqPX8pXXUsWsuK7SiQE2khsmHbYlFMyC8tEYMVhwpaKg-WtMkhZb7pKBpjBw6Nqw=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0T0cCmhES3wcX26isBFEg5NxPNgA8ZngMF-ny37rT0PAQYkyW9uxRIEZjgRoht3h8YRC7y86G9cPgtGsIVS45PI-VHtzDbfnEBTr5quukMQ3JrUs46oSPlXH3QTj3pl7Obn6mx=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3soeoQGZBpVsxcOHfnn2yzn7P-fBje1BltzyLXsdWEKjXIaxYThJcvGXpK506AupSpMLcJe0sgWhbmfGoDjelPd2pdP7Hq0GBCtBvLumIO34WEU0C8yN7gUjJ3FHdjXtkIbCfXnA=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1wirENKPqu_cvXQYvpteszWajYThQXVE5vUnOITm_FvAPiwb7MISnEpw9q9RvcG5-gvKm8lt_zlZpaoE9MnxtMwRr_D6PK4epqPIqdKOX-HwUfq0QzEJJts31dGLnH-CfdgWc=k-no"],"When":"2023-7-1"}]

quite a number of years. After hearing mixed reviews regarding the food, we were quite unsure on what to expect, however, with high hopes, we still decided to visit.

Started off with their chicken tikka and the masala roomali roti. Didn’t disappoint. The chicken tikka was soft and very well seasoned. It was one of the very few I have had that doesn’t require the squeeze of lime to accentuate the taste. Paired with the roomali it was a started to get us started.

Moving on we had the butter naan along with the chicken tikka masala. Another stellar combo. Hot, fluffy naans with perfectly spiced chicken tikka masala with that perfectly caramelised onion flavour, something you should definitely order.

Next we moved to the chicken biryani. It’s a light biryani. Not too oily not too heavily reliant on array of spices. Simple yet tasty. Perfect lunch is what I would say.

[{"Name":"Swagat Patil","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUUt3zJ8G02qadRndtUZm59d1uBmsR-4qyXKfba1JpdCvBxnPkw=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had stopped their for lunch with family.
Amazing Atmosphere, Hygenic Place.
The service is amazing as well.
Dishes are over priced but delicious. Potion sizes are good.
1. Pollo Arabiata Pizza - Super tasty pizza, Loved it. Fresh cherry tomato add little kick in taste.
2. Kids Chicken Fingers - Taste was amazing, potion soze was too small. Certainly not a dish worth 700 bucks.

Service is top notch.

Mandatory 10% addition of tip in bill is something I did not like. Guests should decide whether to tip or not & how much to tip. Adding flat 10% in bill is not required. If they want to charge it in bill it should be declared in their Menu.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0GThGJiBu1BpThOfIW4fTeOfWg9cq3qa7v0ezOb4iROQgJlYl36mebMZFhSr6a54pq1sqR55MqLwoYH1VeFeZ0wQYg9xTZQFFb8xmB8-ywqp8o2PRBfEvAZZ-5POf96XgM9aVgdHERLUA=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3te7xInjGX56pNPF0HL3Mx8xplH_BGPA-tsr5WYNSSwMU5sXsKZkOWByJTzjtrd8I6mddvGnV8HfCJggptnFo_j_5oQ8575AKP7L2gzczmJno2MbCWSuDhRLZjyJs3Ll1uuaQI7tX9i8Q=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3Lp5ieZZe2b72qz-NbhrZ0Y_r1k57k4w1m1Iaymr_MV3lfQxpps28eRr0yR7P-jvyEJudYTWNcGDSpedniMX6mlzuWrC8Ic0iYxzXu-kgZAYbZ7slqdycQe0eufye1PCREDe9iOkMizeRI=k-no"],"When":"2026-2-16"},{"Name":"Govind Sharma","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWWNSZnnjqQLB9nPMlIza8GYDA-sGhPhTQyLsNf1gq8xQLkJsRb=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Pizza By The Bay is truly one of Mumbai’s most iconic dining destinations, offering the perfect combination of great food, vibrant ambience, and an unbeatable sea-facing location on Marine Drive. The moment you step in, you’re welcomed by a lively, energetic vibe that instantly sets the mood for a memorable dining experience.
The biggest highlight is, without a doubt, the stunning view of the Arabian Sea. If you’re lucky enough to get a window-side seat, the experience becomes even more special—watching the waves while enjoying your meal is pure Mumbai magic. Whether it’s breakfast with the morning breeze, an evening meal with city lights, or a monsoon visit with rain and waves in the backdrop, the atmosphere here is always captivating.
The food quality is consistently good and flavorful. Their pizzas are perfectly baked with rich toppings and bold flavors, making them one of the best pizza spots in the city. Pasta, garlic bread, sandwiches, and breakfast options are equally satisfying. Both vegetarian and non-vegetarian dishes are well prepared, and the menu offers enough variety to suit different tastes, though a few items may feel premium-priced for the portions. Desserts and beverages like hot chocolate also stand out.
The ambience deserves special mention—antique-style décor, warm lighting, and a classy yet relaxed crowd give the place a timeless charm. It’s suitable for everything from solo visits and casual meet-ups to professional meetings and weekend outings. The place does get quite crowded, especially during peak hours and weekends, and waiting times can be long, but the experience is worth the patience.
Service is efficient and friendly, with staff guiding you well through the menu and attending promptly despite the crowd. While the lively atmosphere can make it a bit noisy, it adds to the overall energy of the place rather than taking away from it.
Overall, Pizza By The Bay is a must-visit for anyone who loves good food paired with a spectacular sea view. Slightly on the expensive side and often crowded, but the location, vibe, and quality make it a memorable 5-star experience. Perfect for creating beautiful Mumbai memories.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn10anlMaOwU0bhOvC2P9J8eq7kRyMOVbL-ePgdAf9QlAylF3AXOuc3zy4tkgTudKnBmanadZmy9k-PARP86pbuXulMHq1rfSBBA2nTt6SNduHJ_bt7wip4lo2MDH3pxPVttnQn59I_hfHA=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3s8HtOUMts4uk5SzPA-9Rbhh8nCKOlpTlMhr_0bpwWagkc1kWdouBySJOWPQat6E7dZi9gk9nweQG26Eo2XDOsr5XvO_pT8ZbV8RQM-S865gRifdcLtZPOhzspIJsZ3kptzNdQOSSU5fTA=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1uWivmF6Gv3EfJ-_WOyG7UR_60OWvo56EP_dbgqYMO-pxG13Ffe2y6ODn-S83e3QOvvjiXsxz62ErGyqNXmU1g_TEYNhp8yac0QILutGVkfcxpK3xzcXIfq2Tyx5HL1H79qFc7e6_tjAn8=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0285rTkpMzfqdZa9lPX67XrKNT8Em2KuN081XBVFcQVtGQ5QfoRQcfKHF2OM4kN713F8M86x3ycrNuIlyaq1K52Quba5hepT3P0hLa9BtYyo_EunEFM7jKRho1pVQvmgbfkYRAcs6x5z0=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0wj2TEnTlFej2DD7NwKqySV10XOkjPUacJp8Eq1WSs5LiNOmgPKfLeEDNoG0GClyaI6l6Fz3Q0LLMiuHa343mH9yQKfOQy5QfK4xBrZnhOt5NoO5ITQN-Ol5CNIhtsTny0xd7TG50lqSBz=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1uxYpOUwxQ4MNGe_z0XMsJtQbTk3zoZgZAGljRyQs5NC9uxOLjocRwhhU-MbAFr3oSD8VRC3rwxZ_ijVy_-poQonEr8wOz0idAqrTnQdmBWL2p4hvupW6AiAyg9v8N81iHUxehlkrxVLYO=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1CVLf_n41jSQbHo0mGtaXMg5XxWwsmIDvsj0JnV50fJD55wMX_sE1p5fnSlfGuUqtaGijO4rvvi_e7T4c49q878y8BymyBPA8AImvIsVtAqu3VkJi3nZzCEE1gD5PTL55zAWLevUjewAc=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2mxB_Kz78AWCWls6IlhQ4PIAgt-HiSHpmdXvhNFl4Ub33oZ1XcvCFXk0qIrWHWDiqgn4_I92F4qPGVvPMTDxlatqwpoYlGIDnjGH-Y6p78oKVmAStskcOG_hvqyufGonAtYb8dTVsDanUH=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1vEP0GDgGCfeakI8xPZdZgKvp7DJEF_KbU1Pp0UGDuIwO5vkUc_STk2cSvJ9IJkPbY1M4OkP1OjG3q9gt7Pg2jpgPBriQSQ2Sa2Bv5-AOqGEtIcMWB5Sxd6uKQ2P0c56hUkrwW_UAFB-YL=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2DsHpGk2OT8La_po84qO93jePETp5ugV992bFKlb-KEkekQs4OXxP-0DM-fQJJrPleYSZSQc6gockLpxF7zGpEuaDjMFWkUHMTrG_xu8-hv8wQE4lA4MXudmxDsNwfxzI17SKlVviomKw The location itself is unbeatable – right opposite Marine Drive, with a perfect view of the sea that makes dining even more special.

I tried their **half-and-half veg pizza**, and it turned out to be an excellent choice. The base was thin, crisp, and baked just right – not chewy or soggy. On one half, I went for the **classic veg toppings** (tomatoes, capsicum, onions, and olives), which had a fresh and vibrant flavor. The other half was **loaded with gourmet toppings**, with a rich cheesy layer, mushrooms, and bell peppers that gave a nice savory balance.

What stood out was the **quality of the cheese** – creamy, stretchy, and not too oily – and the **balance of spices and herbs**, which kept it flavorful without overpowering the freshness of the vegetables. Portion size is good enough to share if you’re not a heavy eater.

The **ambience** is calm and elegant with the sea breeze adding charm. Service was decent and staff courteous, though the place can get busy, especially in the evenings. Pricing is slightly on the higher side, but the taste, quality, and overall experience justify it.

✅ **Highlights**:

* Great sea-facing view
* Perfectly baked thin crust
* Fresh veggies & quality cheese
* Half-and-half option gives a variety in one pizza

⚠️ **Minor Cons**:

* Slightly pricey
* Gets crowded during peak hours","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn13aqsNtFFjjpZypDv0nkkU1vRxBeFM_qHJkFoYadML80CQdYNabNI3JK4L0IEW805ba2iKGuL3rOZVK5SMM-NZeZm1ltlzEYUuCGSb2v-5evWEPU5fgw0iAR2pcc7KdYrhxWkzqZGGgQs=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3r_JswCxS_i-o36i2h6wsOPkney6QS74QCDaHwGs1KP4LML2S-RRNp_d9LQadPT21sCmRI5fjTtXt76J-Aq-5EDO7Yq14-GqMqkHXO2e62s7VSfxsii-71dZWaAOhW6hSyJEYINoFhS-U=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3CByCU1hivu_q0S-f7kvj5dRp96EQdrxtrhKANs2Q9VGHKMInOmIj-0towFwUk_CCIy3ICu6qMwZF0k_0VLr4xdV1YVpLef9py9QiW4rTbHSlLkDboaCsJDdbv9NPsSEQr7v-kfs6Pj-xP=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2ODMEGL37YXOQfp_CR2zqEmHAqMpAGMPPC74aO_aCXCq6GSsAeoS_d2BhGrwZG5rmin590rPJORtjdMcpEAhae02rQfhvbPCKc5DTYzmrznwkC2c1p8t_rPeHx1nOnEWGzvo_nW3iehF8N=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2BnW8d7aX9_opvp__ET2s_nSSFxer4NynMJMyYKw8MeAYS1WerLmeB3s1CU8l9JPom14q6qWwBA1u2dtk2BGhCDJIavhGDxyJjNflzW8aRWLSFbeijfxo7AbwKyWFrBWapkxgJAgyu-m5q=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn20UwX1-JX-0prZ58u_gjKnN814Z_uqoDE8BclA9atDsQKsIh9dnAIgoaJ_oVOdrFblLnk4UVfbEQ_vO0cnAr0IdI1Ow-KP7dv38HWi4csGHokPUCpkzKTd9i6qlZZSpNAQtSNxm9OYqFos=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2a07tuRx1v9ttZZSjmbvz_epu9DXzpE1KpZDeajsuXn1-eRTySa4DSk2mGr6MQh7Yy9xYqPodnjfyqhLIdmD9n-9v58rwf_V5bkFcRMJ_CeKNvUXTGne4mH6RyukW57hO4JvUq61iwEsc=k-no"],"When":"2025-9-7"}]

Situated in Soho House on Juhu Tara Road, this restaurant boasts an enviable location overlooking Juhu Beach. The ambience is undoubtedly the highlight; the decor is tastefully curated, perfectly capturing a premium vibe.

Cons: While the menu offers authentic Italian flavors, the portions are notably small. Unfortunately, our experience was marred by inconsistent service and also we had to proactively request the complimentary bread basket and olive oil dip.

I tried the Margherita pizza, which was absolutely delightful,simple, perfectly done, and full of flavor. The penne pasta with veggies was decent, but the real star for me was the salad with rocket leaves and pear. Fresh, bright, and beautifully balanced… easily my favorite dish of the day.

That said when it came to service there were a few highs and lows which I believe alters the overall experience but more on that later.

The food was our highlight for this place;
Arancini, crispy fried and served on a base of really great sauce which balanced the richness of arancini with just enough tart flavour.

The Vegetable Lasagne, well this was like the star and quite literally, the cook of the pasta the tomato sauce n the healthy topping of melted cheese all of it made this one dish feel like literal comfort simply, difficult to quite explain in words but anyone who visits here should give this dish a try, you won’t regret it😁.

Spinach and Ricotta Ravioli has always been like my go to a safe dish for a new place and they nailed it right, light sauce with cherry tomatoes fried basil leaves and properly seasoned filling of ravioli.

The deserts have a separate menu at this place and they actually deserve it so, we had a Lemon and Ricotta cake, honestly a really nostalgic experience with this one it was trip down the memory lane and the Tiramisu the layers the taste of coffee the rich layer of mascarpone truly delicious.

On the account of drinks we got a few different ones; Iced Mocha, Cafe Mocha, a cute mocktail with a hint of watermelon and a sprite😅

I first visited Melting Pot in 2014, and really liked their food, I visited after a long time. The place looked beautiful, we reached around 7pm to celebrate a friend's birthday, on a Saturday evening and we had booked the table as there were 9 of us.
The service was average, and food felt average too. Will I go there again? Mostly no, at this price point there are many other amazing places available.
As I had vegetarian food, this review is based on that. I think my non-veg friends liked their food more.
The menu felt too busy, it was difficult to really decide what to order. One cute thing is they use Red glasses for non-veg and green for vegetarians
✅Food: 3.5/5
Vegetarian Food is average
Chicken Manchow Soup: Friends had ordered it, and they liked it.
Veg Lemon Coriander soup: I had distinctly remembered having this soup on my first visit, and really loving it. This time it was ok ok. Too thick I would say.
Dingri Lasooni (Mushroom): This was really tasty.
Hara Patta Aur Matar ke Sheekh: This was too big, for a starter, it was not bite sized, also it was too dry.
Peshawari Kofta: it was decent, we had it with roti
Masala Papad roasted: this was good
Drinks: Ginger Bliss, Blue Sea, Lemon fizz, Spice Guava, Only spice guava was great, others were too sweet, had to get them changed
Chicken Fortune Pot Rice: My friends liked it.
✅Service: 4/5 Service was fairly decent
✅Ambiance: 5/5 The place looks beautiful, seating is fairly spaced out,
✅Value: I would not really visit again, we ended up spending about 1000 per head, which was expensive for the taste","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Kunal C","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXvJCShLaYep6ECYGbsMd_skdjkKOUtA0G7PC1Wu6xjLr3VLqb59g=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Awesome food. BORN

A vibrant, nostalgic journey through the heart of Bandra, blending a cool-urban aesthetic vibes.
The space opens into a high-energy  millennial living room featuring graffiti-adorned walls, dim lighting, and a curated playlist of old-school hip-hop and rock that sets a buzzy, informal tone.

• Malai Podi Toast with Stracciatella: A savory, inventive spin on the classic cheeni malai toast, featuring podi butter and creamy stracciatella served on cumin-flecked focaccia.

• Grandma’s Fanny Prawn Dish: A heartfelt homage to local heritage, this dish recreates a traditional family recipe, offering robust, home-style flavors presented with modern technique.

• Posh Hersch Puff: An elevated take on the humble Bandra chicken puff, where flaky pastry is "drowned" in a luxurious, umami-rich truffle and wine sauce.

• Venus Jam Cake: A hallowed tribute to the iconic local Venus Bakery, this moist cake is served with a mixed berry sorbet to balance its nostalgic sweetness.

The overall experience is designed to feel like a love letter to the neighborhood, making it an excellent choice for those looking for innovative small plates that pack a significant umami punch","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2xrxjNAslC2K72UKoWnDOb8qSdnz3dbWiTn88xPyHEheyzv3pG00MS8WmZH1UfvOa8epI9vff3PKrNMCo-bXTwSuTRIVAGg0ydqaBeoK4qaUphYVuggR8iDTh7S3jvQNzmozVdxoaD51n_=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn21_KsVgq-KUr2z8qLJcb_W7DfFP0mvE7rzDYcNEQ3xw2A-MRlYniZMCdhfdPLIsnHMhnbDwGktlsq9x3rS-SwE_dDqIUgy-NH7518bgpztItfzToNYrmuqaOxHUlaJFRmI0gW1QpmRJ2I=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn14w0RQg6_nmrjELK5zBy1bD9NMzK4JGI14P5Z_c0QgLUBnqOduS54ZTYSB5b0ZSHxmOgBXadTfYWd47QhNyP2s9uWkGD1bymdrWA9IjaoUt2usQDg1Dk27yL1l_8QghIHvr2x1-KYEDtLy=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3dHKb_jT0zrFPTcLEabSnQPj3bQfa0-R22Bam5nexLHnBRCXEsrUFCjhkYeNQD94wg9MDKsLZ3c_1U35kTG1gq2gqzX1KpPxWGroJDwk6jQ9D8NYzznH8pl66sLd_FFnQknhYk1r-zre5N=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3PveHPbXb0ftsbXoAOc5xqOHd25UqdRY3FP9NMexXoq_jjW4i10-M8f7Owd9Iq4AqtEFQUnvw_QE8XYlsoWGVW8IJPDz-INZCx_DBoMIUHjcIshKf_z1991Rew0wVpUcF7q-Gx9MjTwHmh=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn14_c7qZxU-20TIUE-me6ooyLRtu6vjld4LhiXuTcCjbLws563JmRCMRSaJV2Tr4o1eiK6_Nbir40OC_y61XaSzJDAx_vJcJtUQrEqZfXtMaRf8_hrJ0nhhcNOLE_Dp2XkY8W9izmYYGFMa=k-no"],"When":"2025-12-23"},{"Name":"Omar Ismail","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjX9cwhEQw0PIhBXC957XsmoNYR5ghMHrmj5GUB3ioRVTH7EqTg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"A really cool place to chill and enjoy a really good meal. Have a chat with the bartender and he’ll explain where the liquor comes from, how long the flavor has to sit in a dungeon before being released, and maybe give you a taster if you’re nice enough.

Imagery aside:

- the food tastes great;
- the portions are surprisingly large for similar restaurants in Mumbai (which does make up for the price somewhat);
- the drinks are memorable (unless you have too many); and
- the ambience is fine - it’s on a busy Bandra street though so I think they’ve done well with what they had. You can still see Salt Water Café’s bones sticking through the walls (the place that was here before Bandra Born was…born)

[{"Name":"Prashant “Paddy” Demello","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW9-s2T0L1V_YJGjIks4PArRY5mqUI6_qdIqsMUy7bWwQL8AYmf=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Fusion Ville is a place that might appeal to a very specific crowd, but unfortunately, it did not work for me at all. I visited this place hoping for a relaxed environment where one could sit quietly, enjoy a couple of drinks, and unwind after a long day. Instead, the overall experience felt quite sad, dull, and uninspiring, which really took away from the purpose of visiting a bar or lounge.

The vibe of the place is the biggest letdown. There is a noticeable lack of warmth or energy. The ambience feels flat, almost lifeless, and there’s nothing about the setup that makes you feel comfortable or welcome as a lone guest. If you are someone who enjoys a peaceful drink with a calm atmosphere, soft music, or even a sense of privacy, this place will likely disappoint you. Rather than feeling relaxing, the environment feels awkward and disconnected.

As a lone drinker, the experience felt especially uncomfortable. There was no sense of attentiveness or effort from the staff to make solo guests feel at ease. The service was mechanical — orders were taken and delivered, but without any courtesy or engagement. A bar should be a place where even a single person can feel relaxed and unjudged, but here it felt quite the opposite.

The music and overall mood did not help either. Instead of creating a soothing or uplifting environment, the background sound added to the dullness. It neither energised the place nor offered calmness. This combination made the experience feel long and uninteresting, even though I wasn’t there for too long.

Food and drinks were average at best. Nothing stood out in terms of taste or presentation. The menu itself might look decent on paper, but the execution lacked finesse. For the pricing, one would expect better flavours or at least consistency, which was missing. Drinks were served without any attention to detail, making the experience feel rushed rather than enjoyable.

Overall, Fusion Ville feels like a place that hasn’t clearly decided what it wants to be — a lively hangout, a quiet bar, or a social space. Unfortunately, it fails to deliver strongly on any of these fronts. If you are coming with a large group and are only focused on basic food and drinks, you might manage. However, if you are a solo visitor looking for a calm, comfortable space to enjoy a drink, this is not the place for you.

Two stars only because the place is functional, but beyond that, the experience lacks soul, warmth, and comfort.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2V0Kh-THX-ceCQG_1PyN9sim4vweqem4cSIb0qNpnCzgy8ov5sxWgBnSenG7ZNKtZG5UVelpmYdo_40XXgPvKH9erentCy-ZHLApuskSChDlPzU4hJ-PnikvavW9T9WKxVjwUZdp4nZweu=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1cz2KLEAXhJIvlQ-MSj1XNUCgkv9sRlOq8YcruJ3HX21Dw33LAPHGiPN6z6ngMdJJyZ-hfymzuLAIyGT0bm97gC-DrYQ-bj5lb3SPqUh83UQXgS7MCnIzfn1wnsJK1dcaEQuSeJUwSUoKy=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0MJpn36Rl9dyG2HjjDe8CrBuv3SLaD4L9jn6SpOpqVS6ufV8KeoHq7z4l2YE7HU35i0lKEkhuMkPL3ciItJrN6Tr2w0AV3aEa5G3303LlBrSP1q9j5131JEoxOHrdas63bDXpRygCcUK5G=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn15kHZNKfmpxpEPJZgwW8aAnlWLnqDgL4WZxA6_gm2-K6jQr1Q1n0tTmizhJu6Zqx_0T2z8JSwaPGwq-j5CQdKIibkp637WcSZQ06nFj3c0MX4icyaBE9fhO7-9k_5_tkevgoE3Xe4r8TXT=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3NIZyqkIQQ1zCK3OCQE8W2sJxBLijvZy5dYB5dE_U1ptOPWQUoWrawFeF1xG8CdwInESXk_vP05Kb4glgy5sfcOdrjfeSs4IA3_K2quEzsI8JxDRecEm9YPZZaGU7TAxFrP-cQ3l2yre4=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn325wIbFrnlg741tNL1zdlJe7wGsE03GGuCfFpe4ZisbI0W-yBw3mWSC3ArsXbAKJ8Ip_nhmdhRazhY6okzaZkuwsQiPeYRTeYSmR3IK-iGLi6BlAfO83QfodaB9ss7Fm3MkziEyf_20Mo=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn22109moHk9LpGpwd9cKibYE6hOzhFTi5nSfZX1SOLGLhqRES5VJZuyMQg0E2Ld71rgD9U5fGsGt4bQ2axsexkA8TVW43vL_NGwDpEBBG09Sb2egmHa_1YOrlyCSCVj1xHZQQA4HPfWFF8=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1lI5Ggl36Zbdmh72px47BDoXKyWzqrsN4cPbF2YhYtUxRTnN8q2JhA6iiQ4sFgz15S3DRSCC0mKX118SeTo9P4VLWKIrqEUbI7C8vMJvC1_jPmuUF5m5IrYLdKQU0PZQ1Vi-tkEjFDf5ER=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn070onRoDM5Nif5f-nV3jFS6kIoAmeLJS9YJFwhR1CoDHyY9ztWqu53bSqwJtxAaXiVUsJyEEiWwfsVKa4L94jNrSDWLE0zrM-0_U1oBDwA9rmd7X6bdIZm9APHvXfRrGuSRoJ5TurVuojW=k-no"],"When":"2025-12-21"},{"Name":"Tejas Our delightful odyssey commenced with Tandoori pomfret, Galouti kebab, Malai chicken tikka, and prawn, each dish a brilliant testament to the chef's innovative genius. The main course showcased their legendary crab Sukkha and Kali Dal, masterfully crafted to exhilarate the senses. The crowning glory was the paan kulfi and chocolate roshogolla, desserts that left us utterly enchanted. The food was truly sublime, with flavors that joyfully danced on the palate and desserts that stole the spotlight, rendering the entire experience profoundly unforgettable.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3_2HZ3KvlG_R34en3LG8g2ezzMj42ZGflUHJl6W7-BxI219MtmGi3Ko_UfJZJvsYIe6pdRt_mwDelPWe9LYoTiUWz5Zc3L3u8Sa3jgAktslE99xifiGr5-99x7qk9hd6VjguWWhAB5-a_4=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3xHrh1iVzLZnZsFoZT5zy6RiDQjF1vvCBGo0i98L6RaeVx71B3s3fpaFS1qYBZWQcZ_jmcG0Yn-rkayfBfwEZxT3LfDWt4CS7pkeO8VC_LCfMrUQOk_GY6vWjbrxLRRhbzjnfpfSZyfk1C=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1paat2XC_RUsAR0Zybqv-AiUPnsqfWfUkMB3tdGPw8yZ5njHGRk_gsy5cWidGgHH3N78f3DK-0JYlG5Syav2nxwRjTt1_d-zSAhXq5Xwe4Ke2UwOZ2yo1VPIt-R1eR5KN_VASprWEN-p7Y=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3sNarPktDlK7HyCEpMJDutwqdeKfIc00iU5xwhTCuBtACaPbyRBN7651CU-8o8ll0HEXOINWXEIaOHsARK4Pw0jxGC1GS1_ye3NtLXZWCgnAWlTJ5EixaMAbdKvTJCULW11Z5jMPD4NNdc=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2w8qHH-6lpXO7CsBsGG9--zqiSGvZ9Q0mncqhKZ3WwYSjSWvfOQEd_mj7qBOHUTrdjrwF6mKagzw81ELo4g-ksBUBGedNfJIUG4Q_9t1GSF-tU4E8A8KoUa2RU2y2iF8ktfNpJgfXZ_ps=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0TK5GZ3VASnfooLSG69ZqPDL-wxzqtHtXwWkvNMczJn7S_h4q-VaLaCedfbRXJJ0N9-ajB7dCQGZnfLbZiwlMJdfN8Z71QqzB-QL6m4_o5MjiCMo57FBpKjQ7jcu7X4mCgDEnK---vSONC=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1emn1ATCWxncPf4ab3cGx3Wc_tYFxdwnGh6b6gfoxct7mQ5nLqOc6gfNsdNfPuq5df8gZuIvVD9Kb76HJqas7eGaz0wEaSXfwo3GvC6jUp8NLkvvhsS_RZQfvrWKaQJQM1tXovQgOYDA73=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3K-DdqNSm2lid_vvnnlfYBZwyXZYeaSEPTWlvWi5laE07uZ0YcdeeqkbiuJxBnMkkes_8xHQfIpuDEopK_5Fn1NrcTzUJg4A1wNtJ_39d1d6EYcnTJK2XCdVIlutggZBH3rpkySP6Lw0la=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2V0EpYBjqYBjTwCmVLyTH4-fWOlC86cq6QrsCKWge9EIy6JkIrn9-UlzFD9oeZx8_MXY75jPrVX_TnHFKkFE-PPZULTHDAVcm6pk5q_HSXzK1NYtQ8gSsLQhNLqysCTdz47OpGMJJ7jVQD=k-no"],"When":"2026-2-25"},{"Name":"Aakash","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjW5sB75N8WXvgEGdR5SJ3oJEoXG-HHkyJ8yJeJn7z0U9wYUeOdQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":4,"Description":"There’s an unmistakable old-world charm here; an almost nostalgic, lived-in elegance that feels refreshingly authentic in a city that often chases the new and shiny.

The ambience is wonderfully understated: warm lighting, vintage touches, wooden furniture that has seen stories, and that classic Bombay colonial-meets-Bandra vibe.

The food is rich, deeply flavoured, cooked with real care. Every dish carries that unmistakable depth; nothing is timid or overly polished. The spicing is confident, the portions generous, and the execution consistent. It’s the kind of meal that lingers in memory long after you’ve left.

Whether it’s a quiet celebration, a catch-up with old friends, or simply craving something soulful, Pali Bhavan delivers exactly what one hopes for in a neighbourhood favourite: character, flavour, and comfort in equal measure.

Highly recommended for those who appreciate substance over spectacle.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2yOimXwGFXXnBFrr4ZygNBBDfvSAKNpDKdwYkzTY1IBz1_hKYJhImDsGCQurFXVP-EE9B_7dwUcqKXQPVQPFc_USl4941ykp_A143z-Gr-j6NDM3tZZaxChorxwlW5FsfdJUF71QAce4M=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1-RmrorvxG7raH6Pxqa9CWb-WHN7dmRwuxURKlkysVJZTWAjgrlesrhaIs2_RlhyvcrFpM8LEpk29K6VgEFTdYnhaWf70t_J4gCHLfx5gxeS0yEmqReOT75wZjSAwHp0BFHceJKxD7nVA=k-no"],"When":"2026-3-2"},{"Name":"Lewis Crombie","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWNHgmjO9kpAetfBOb-TwbrYDG8GYTGe8EHAcYl5LPhfvufOZ0=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Simply the best Indian food I've ever eaten. [{"Name":"gaurav haralkar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjWtdcpG1inIElINyoxf6ezVRH7C-ye4dYQngPaDbj8viUBqUexA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Had an awesome time along with my gang here last night. Each & every dish we ordered was really good & worth it.
Surmai tawa fry was good & topped with fried garlic had nice homely taste,slice was huge but thinly cut.
Prawns butter garlic was yummylicious,fresh,not heavily coated with corn flour kept the prawns juicy & succulent inside,sauce was too good & garlicky.
Chkn lollys were garama garam,crisp on the out tender n juicy inside,perfectly fried not too oily at all also schezwan sauce was not very oily.
Chkn kalimiri kebab was tender,well marinated & perfectly grilled not rubbery or chewy like some hotels do.
Mutton sukka was pretty good too not much cocconutty
Chkn ghee roast & mutton handi along with soft,luscious neer dosas was an amazing combo & finger licking good.
Whiskies & beers are reasonably priced.
Regular chakna like chakli,bhurji & half fry omlettes were just perfect.
Service is warm & welcoming,thanks to Mr.Pawan & service team for taking care of everything.

A very special thanks to the owner Mr.Mahesh Shetty 🙏🏻for welcoming us & looking after all the small & big things so that we had a great [{"Name":"manish kakad","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjVbU-3961tr0RUUVS8aEyp-3ocFmlHEYFjDtSzUwb5JNRgwo_uWzg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Boat Club is a vibrant, nautically-themed restaurant that successfully balances a casual dining feel with a high-energy lounge atmosphere.

The standout feature is undoubtedly the decor. True to its name, the interior incorporates boat-shaped seating and maritime elements, creating a unique "sailing" aesthetic that sets it apart from typical mall eateries. During the day, it is family-friendly and relaxed, but as the evening progresses, the energy shifts with live music and upbeat lighting, making it a popular spot for birthday celebrations and group hangouts.

The menu is impressively diverse, spanning North Indian, Continental, and Chinese cuisines. A must-try for vegetarians is the Paneer Tikka Pizza. Unlike generic mall pizzas, this version features:
The Crust: A well-baked base that provides a sturdy crunch without being overly doughy.
The Toppings: Generous chunks of paneer that have been properly marinated in traditional tandoori spices, giving the pizza a distinct smoky flavor.

The service is generally hospitable, though it can get a bit slow during peak weekend hours or when live performances are in full swing.

Whether you’re stopping by after a movie at INOX or looking for a lively dinner spot, The Boat Club is a solid choice for its creative theme and reliable fusion dishes.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1VgYW9AIuiGB-SwCOzpiADvFcwb6z2xdkSzmryvJ6D1KVqEANm4Karxx0cT2u3eLdT1PN69faR2O9d6VsEapAN5IY9nCt217DOMNk0sKrHfyYMfF_ZjAAGGO9FoxipVswHcuI89qx-D_5v=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0nt1E1h8wgastCC0RSD1uAvfagDul8f4JCyrR_JDytYPSwyPpjDFfNw7SF9kVuT42tHMHAabBLfmJF0TNh0L-MJpjCtTWtrLawlwDFGGosirBqiwhkfBIgsoVRgWMcWJQ2yn3SX_bvfZo=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1r_Vr3H5nl-06GjoMp6BY6E8NPyPcUU1hUO2qjblYNH8ZBhpmhxKZJlL6pqX0_8h7wMk6R6SY0fyV08OiU7_qzvUw381W3JYBi4F3OXoG-N_0f7N4avORAb10WrTzEn4DgdOi8iCqnWzA=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1di7xAFFXPsVZAcqm-wsLUEmCzQUD16xUt_JlZ-5B2bpr276AbtHxrRiYYVNi_wYxXzo_pgVtZNr6zCQ-oh8fDO0ww51izBgrOpIrm6jCHt85NsMhKhmc6HZI14iGlLGmVQiU8oxK6br7T=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0rhG-BmiyUHhlDr9KEeeClF7-eb9RIiiCD_ELAzcNdQrgH9R16NjR0fcyQ8rKdIdjHpO5khtlah_2p3wkDU9lAtcDXrFgYId9jYgJn9dc5HOgLPfXo-qjrPSJFwDKKK8Js9CpjUvKf8mie=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3BwzEb0gfVjEoLmq28tmvjBVcrH7wWuxKWmugt2gpZpwRub1ui5Cb61GcrsCJ6l819V5-60ucjinmlvBCj4BiLfQgguMFImGbmtQVf1O0Fp9metWA2d_fPGbndB-1wkAA4MtD0TAYegjM=k-no"],"When":"2026-1-5"},{"Name":"Siddhesh Prabhu","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXzKJziOrshz6kJW9nNZSndh02iruppl3Yh0KuZa4fSiMt17qdp=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Looking for a restaurant with family, then hop in here.

We were seated as soon as we arrived. Another family was seated around the same time on a dirty table. We ordered and a few minutes later. That table were upset that their table wasn't cleaned and they weren't served yet. So there was an argument between them and a staff member that could be heard across the dining room.

The food came quickly. My father in law asked for a side of chillis but it never came even though we heard the waiter remind another waiter. At the end when I went to pay the waiter assumed I couldn't understand him and wrote tip at the end of the bill. Kept insisting on a tip even though he wasn't the one who served us. And I would normally leave a tip but due to the pushing and rudenss I didn't.

Although food came quickly we had to keep asking to order. The food came and we had no service apart from this. Although staff were intentive they lacked friendlyness

The food was amazing best biryani I've had from my visits in India. with family.
The service was good.
The food was awesome. The Banarasi Dahi ke Gular was a very good appetizer , however I did find the quantity to be lesser than expected. Only 3 pieces is way too less. For mains, we had Purvanchal Ka Saag along with Missi Roti and Garlic Naan. The saag tasted amazing. Both naan and roti were soft. Once again I found the quantity to be a bit less than expected. Next we ordered the Hyderabad Veg Dum Biryani with Black Daal. Loved the Biryani, it was perfectly flavoured. The black was was good too. For dessert I ordered the Kulfi Falooda, the Kulfi was nice, tasted great with the Rabdi added on top. Since it was my birthday, they offered a complementary Chocolate cake at the end. The cake was well decorated, extremely thick and rich. While leaving the restaurant, we were all offered Paan at the door by the bartender. I would definitely recommend this restaurant to others.
I’ve reduced 1 star because there were a few mosquitos where we were seated.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2Ntv4b5YIp4ZJAigLKMcQM8Kq2tdYQcA-nZ75CZv4PKOqLTpevqai2H1Kx2GHGpcdif0RoWhf2A6wAVz1POZouNKIromxIuyg-8fhyrxNxrXfdlkevzOxCiCrlIeOqjJKmna2p2wSLz49o=k-no"],"When":"2026-1-23"},{"Name":"Debashis Pati","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXehdUiGWpl54SvjiBPOXeZX92bcuOU44s-6695vAahuBkbV5I=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The sahib room & kipling bar is awesome, Stepped into The Sahib Room & Kipling Bar last Friday and felt like I'd time-traveled to the Raj era—think polished teak panels, ceiling fans lazily stirring the air, and walls adorned with sepia-toned maps of old India. The vibe is effortlessly elegant, perfect for a pre-dinner unwind or a lively catch-up with friends.

The drinks? Outstanding. I kicked off with the signature Kipling Martini—gin, vermouth, and a twist of intrigue that hits just right. My companion raved about the spiced mango lassi cocktail, creamy yet punchy. The bar snacks, like crispy papadum with tamarind chutney, were a flawless nibble to tide us over.

Service was warm and attentive without being stuffy; our bartender, Raj, even shared a quick tale behind the bar's name (nod to Rudyard, naturally). Prices are fair for the sophistication. If you're craving a sophisticated escape with a literary twist, this is your spot. Can't wait to return!","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2LHMTfqTjYNoWu8R9nKKslZroz6uN7pTszLnER4nH9GhtfuMhlMx4UY_IQylJdJs99N5yHNHK7XTFDwb9hM-F2c69l6UytLX1HuKsixhLxhgCXKpYAKT6td3V0gPpJSUY28_x4y5oTJ1JR=k-no"],"When":"2025-10-9"},{"Name":"AJ Jha","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocJVHJCl0zh4Rx2YSGeHoRsvglNd5UpsSqKnn2Lk7OpGFkCx5TU=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Dining at Sahib Room at the St. Regis was truly an unforgettable experience. From the moment we arrived, the warmth and professionalism of the team made us feel incredibly welcome. Chef Taj’s culinary artistry was outstanding—the flavors, presentation, and attention to detail were nothing short of world-class. The dining room boss Kriti, Ganga and the rest of the team went above and beyond to provide impeccable service, ensuring that every part of our evening was seamless and memorable. We also came back for lunch!

This was not just a meal, but a complete experience that beautifully combined exceptional food, elegant ambiance, and heartfelt hospitality. Thank you to Chef Taj, Kriti, and the entire team for making our visit so special—I look forward to returning again soon!","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1OUHt257bPiJ-y0lUOuMpw4M4pdMDvR4r1mhHqXqb4lO7-5sBNfuAwW4luXEFtnY5jp2Huw7NAHPk6_DcBlI7bAJBvm3YllwsLhsvcBFr_dhdGLWMbZW2qD01M9RR7OftHm-FH5qo9wgE=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2L7PvohJJlGR-P1BcT84r7NX4kXbTMhOEEOJW1gdXGfR9zyShSOgNQRsGK5yMlizfC_en75pUz6Pf05dR-JfwnW_H-8nIQ_ARtKqFqTTHM1iL19osiD-5fzuJssZoS8Y4dc9XUuO79tltD=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1P0gpOqQpfEjMp177QeIrIRmfzpx0HY3z4NAQYvd9GdjYJtNSownuXvPaMc8LQNjJmXLQY3DW-jAbW_29cgVR90nKPjW4f5DiB0WV2BhIh2QLvwPLmXCZKEKLvLmgY1v8f8fAStOdlMo2S=k-no"],"When":"2025-8-24"},{"Name":"Roslin Dwivedi","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXY5CEpoALtepRy1xavlNvNVtYui_mOXvIiO9Fg1kF0tWN1AWiX=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"The Sahib Room & Kipling bar Restaurant at St. Regis Mumbai has some delicious Mughlai food for Mughlai cuisine lovers. The restaurant's theme is inspired by the Mowgli story. The staff took us on a culinary safari and they are called by the Mowgli characters. We were served by Cheel and Mowgli. Cheel, the beautiful lady served us delicious dishes like Dhungari Gosht ki Gilawat, Sahib ki Nihari with Khameeri Kulcha and Gosht Biryani. I loved all the dishes as they all had great taste and stood out as unique even if you have one after the other. None of the dishes overpowered the taste. Let’s talk decor, the decor is meant to feel like you are on a jungle safari sitting under the tents. Just that this is sophisticated and has AC and an amazing look to it. Kids loved watching chefs at work through the glass window to the kitchen. The restaurant has dishes for everyone. Kids also loved their kebabs and desserts. [{"Name":"Vishwesh Ranadive","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjXwPWNGX1tVJx-eWjGBiL7wprWV3uLccb0OiT3hV653huaagfzQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":2,"Description":"A Highly Disappointing Maharashtrian Food
Overall Rating: 3/10 (Based primarily on food Taste and facilities)
My recent visit to Vinay in Girgaon was a profound disappointment, particularly for anyone seeking authentic, high-quality Maharashtrian snacks. The experience was marred by poor execution of core dishes and subpar facilities, resulting in one of the worst quick-service dining experiences I've had in a while.
The Food Verdict
The most critical failure of the visit was the Misal Pav.
• Misal Pav: 1/10
This was, quite frankly, one of the worst Misal Pavs I have ever encountered. It lacked the characteristic spice, texture, and authentic flavor profile that defines this Maharashtrian staple. It was sweet 😂. If Misal is your purpose for visiting, please look elsewhere.
• Other Dishes: 3/10 (Okish)
We also sampled the Sabudana Wada, Vatana Pattice, and Veg Biryani. While these items were merely "okish," they still scored a very low 3 out of 10. They lacked freshness, or any distinguishing quality to warrant a recommendation or even a second thought.
Ambiance and Facilities
On a positive note, the restaurant offers good sitting options, including both AC and Non-AC sections, which is a rare plus point in this category.
However, the facility challenges are severe:
• Parking: There is no dedicated parking available, which can be a significant hassle in the congested Girgaon area.
• Toilets: The washroom facilities were, without exaggeration, the worst. This is a major area needing immediate attention and upkeep.
Final Recommendation
If your goal is to experience good, authentic, and delicious Maharashtrian cuisine, do not visit Vinay. You will be better served by traveling a little further to established names.
Alternative Recommendations for Authentic Maharashtrian Food:
• Misal Pav: For a truly great Misal, visit Mamledar Misal (Thane).
• General Maharashtrian Cuisine: For excellent, reliable snacks and meals, Prakash Shuddh Upahar Kendra (Dadar) remains a gold
