[{"Name":"Smruti Rao","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjV5NbnYkMl4b6icfZdHIMRjeMCwEPGMAmYLRXPE2zezcnJK_HoRfQ=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"An exceptional dining experience at Dum Pukht Restaurant.
Truly worth trying the biryani was absolutely exquisite and the kebabs were perfectly crafted, rich in flavor and tradition. The royal ambiance adds a sense of timeless luxury, making the evening even more memorable.
What truly touched us was the thoughtful gesture by the team offering a purse hook with the beautiful Namaste logo. Such attention to detail reflects genuine hospitality and care.
The staff was courteous, warm, and attentive throughout, making us feel valued and welcomed. Dum Pukht is not just about food; it’s about an experience that stays with you. Highly recommended for anyone seeking refined Indian heritage cuisine.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Divya Poddar","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocKEooqy1pVcd6gMpNwWUx1xYzHbCIJXmU2GBRFaBPzrP5YKUA=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Went there for dinner thrice.

The veg biryani, yes the vegetable one, was cooked to perfection. Very mild yet great flavours. It wasn’t a masala overload like the ones served in many places, but the freshness of the vegetables and the sun cooked process were enough to create the magic. This one was good for your taste and tummy.

The aloo gravy was one of the best curries I’ve had- offered the perfect blend of spices, tang and chilli. For those who like chatpata, would highly recommend this.

The after-feel wasn’t a very heavy one. It made me feel light and didn’t upset my stomach (despite all the over-eating I did).

Being eco-conscious, my highlight was-

They mentioned that they don’t waste food, and they keep it aside and offer it to their staff/others.
This was one of the best things I could hear at any fine dining restaurant.","Images":null,"When":""},{"Name":"Bhavesh Tandel","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIPH1tsS_2ooY_UtphHG49vM7nNZRbRC00NHBFed9lu4PMe=s120-c-rp-mo-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Heard a lot about this Restraunt and just had dinner there the food is absolutely bland no spices or whatsoever in their biryani it’s just essence in the food for smell there isn’t any spices can get a better biryani at a fraction of a cost anywhere else’s in Mumbai not worth it at all even the kababs are ok not that great","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn09pXTmzreN8rzQPSCX7Igf9gck0U3TPn5Hnm8_hvDrPXntVWt1kkI1pIu8Mt4jhEvgbF4Tg5nysfxOLQcRNGou7RPEBORikRU8xD1OQgpRHIpoaBCS0XPGsF6kXYUityjrTsujYQ1U_rPr=k-no"],"When":"2026-1-9"},{"Name":"Atharva Bet","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a/ACg8ocIDGnXrSSYhD-8Mgu_U_HSAY-0ksp-Ow2gep2obvYpSh4Nsw197=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"For me, personally, Dum Pukht is the greatest dining room in the world for Awadhi food specifically. The menu is classic, inspired from 200 year old recipes which were cooked in the royal kitchens of Awadh under the rulership of Nawabs. This has been extensively researched by the ITC Master Chefs when they were opening the original Dum Pukht at ITC Maurya and it’s clearly evident in the taste. The Dum Pukht here in mumbai is excellent since Imitiaz Qureshi Ji had shifted to this branch and all the favourites were and are to date faithfully rendered. Current head chef Asif Qureshi Ji is also an excellent chef giving out great dishes consistently. The melt in mouth Kakori Kebab is out of this world, something that has the potential to stay on your palette for years, beautifully seasoned and cooked to perfection it’s a perfect representation of a kebab. Biryani, I have no words. Truly fantastic. It definitely has that ‘Nazakat’ in truest sense. It’s mildly spiced, contains perfectly cooked pieces of prime cuts of lamb and is very fragrant. Culinary craft of the best possible level. The only area where Dum Pukht needs to work is on their curries if I had to critique. The dining room also needs a revamp I guess considering the interiors of other Dum Pukht branches. Rest, I think it’s perfection. Service is also amazing, shoutout to Priya for making my lunch worthwhile. But there’s some scope of improvement in attentiveness of the server, like if my glass of water is almost going to empty, the server should notice and fill it up & thing’s similar to this can be improvised. I took the single diner experience and it was money! Set menus are interesting since they allow unlimited portions in that. Ala Carte is tricky since the prices are overwhelmingly expensive for the portions provided, nothing wrong with the food just the portions can be at least incremented to justify the prices. If you just focus on the Kebabs & Biryani of Dum Pukht at Maratha, you are sure to achieve some sort of culinary enlightenment haha!","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3E90FedXyzx1e4E5hDhVZgZtsr5jF0DtLFcVYOVVzAm9byWp8pnB3CLhl3BmkDN_okTWP-DSyjaHvvLS1N7f0Q5ujXS8MFE3FmS-94bKwcYokwAaTaPHO-R3CMQumTYYzQnCWP=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1jaFTOb-vNcJp6k5Y7YW8lNVBINWNhWurgIlSqV1dvzl_XYh-EXkRyz1qnTzWubpOzb75ffCHIV-7AYR8J0nZX425vrUe6n6hB4nq81MEILwY5_IbqiXpsE_9Z4L-QfZfa8h6f=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2SZhV7YY-xb-vjU2xSMCNYR3sgcGGaCv16ffv-07hkgrppTejpW7RqhX5ksQh66ubNv54fcCwqxTkhYOHs7UiUQNB90qWC0yo2VYB4wuqhH2qGW5h0dFPIaiETKPzqb-uZFXw=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0borlocWLPU8ITU1ibNEOsIaxrWRTPYlnrW_PFNv1WNiFVvWep_cTBpLjvUj9LvirIIlP5Bh4sVVDqw6YZmmkQMG3rfreqUenDlerSZrzSmHV142BBeh1epds_oUirGQLxDYpV=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3bew_0ysC1IpkVB_uIa6-qBaUVUo5E5wu5xnDhabe305J6V_8jPe6TADv2am9wuBx9J31uWUEVS1lTk2HfiuvVDd0_GqFzL6hKiRHTJrqbMoOIL6_WoaCyIy0-JUzxsW-GnpSe=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0rQK3VHUgOSDHCvBbl3K19GHmeTSXNK1RUmLA8WafJo6UMkVAA68toT9zXA3NgN7zetW3KCDKxZgCMeHe8L8iaP2u49NPXDuOdKXrcF2yW0vmanDXFRazaewNQ-KoBgF5XpCaC=k-no"],"When":"2023-3-10"},{"Name":"Sreepriya Subramanian","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjU819EsOUMXUndEE3GvZM4jCRi0DFC_i_9F4-UWvnkjd63nJB9kBg=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":1,"Description":"Seriously terrible service. The smoked crispy pork belly was not too salty (as is often the case with pork in India) and it was indeed tender though it was a shame that it was doused in mayonnaise. The rock shrimp and squid tempura was a generous portion though the batter was just a little too heavy and somewhat disguised the taste of the prawn and squid. We had a prawn okomiyaki, which was very enjoyable though I would have liked to have the option of no bonito flakes, and a little less sauce, that again disguised the taste of the pancake. The chicken tsukune was perfect and one of the highlights of the meal, perhaps bettered only by the buta burra with enoki mushroom, which was divine. The spider roll was another generous portion item and quite good again. Having enjoyed the food so much we went full blast and ordered one desser each. The black sesame ice cream was pretty good without perhaps the subtle flavour of some of the best that we've had but it was certainly better than the warabi mochi, which was a limp disappointment. transcended its origins as a small, cozy sushi bar to become Mumbai’s premier destination for authentic Japanese fine dining. If you are serious about Japanese cuisine, Izumi is a must-visit, though reservations are highly recommended—and often mandatory—due to its compact size and immense popularity.
​The cornerstone of Izumi’s success lies in its unwavering commitment to sourcing the freshest, high-quality ingredients, particularly for its raw preparations.
They serve Sushi, Sashimi, Ramen Bowls, Small Plates, and Grill. I had their Pepper Garlic Veg Chirashi which is a Rice Bowl. Food was delicious.
The restaurant’s atmosphere is warm, energetic, and highly intimate.
Service is generally attentive, quick, and knowledgeable, particularly when asking for recommendations. Be prepared for the premium pricing, which is justified by the quality of the imported and specialized ingredients, but may feel slightly high given the smaller portion sizes on some dishes

As soon as the clock hit 8:30 pm, they switched to softer lighting and started playing the latest music hits on screen along with an acoustic player, which really set the mood.

What we ordered:
• Katsu chicken sushi – 4/5
• Mixed dumplings basket (open chicken sui mai and spicy prawns) – 3/5
• Pad Thai noodles with chicken – 4/5
• Mixed berry cocktail – 5/5 (highly recommend!)

We were attended by Avi, a very kind and smiling gentleman who helped us with seating, recommendations, and honest food suggestions based on our taste buds. The manager was polite and helpful too, very attentive and constantly ensuring every table was comfortable and well-serviced.

⭐️⭐️⭐️☆☆ — Good food, but ambience needs control

We recently visited this restaurant in Andheri and overall the food, service and ambience were quite good. The menu has a nice variety, the dishes were flavourful, and the staff was polite and attentive.

However, one issue really affected our experience. The restaurant allowed a very large group of kids (around 7–8 years old) in the afternoon, and the entire floor became extremely loud. We were a group of six girls seated right next to them, and we couldn’t even hear our own conversation. The kids were running around, shouting, and creating a lot of disturbance.

While I understand kids will be kids, a restaurant should avoid seating such big groups of children together during busy hours or at least manage the noise levels better. It took away from what could have been a peaceful dining experience.

Overall, good food — but the ambience needs better control to ensure all guests can enjoy their meal.","Images":["https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn1vk-JXhDnEuJmOYt9VStO0cXFg3F3uKU212VHpYeWbaSlJQLy13fVaw7qRwVgXxRU2UzRS9SgxZ1Lzk9qdv5DjlmCOk4cOSMy2kqVYcqiievEUU0S7SAIfURskecyx_N09zDaUTX3PcJ4=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3Nb7LAfyFNueZYqyuMTPH509AMGcOqfap-ntEdvjCcKyNy0mdiUoT8adfZGymXt7cbQa43GvmyKsIvs-T5gmESdYqQA1J44GeSi6TnR83A7JWLu5fN6YcMkShTzxZWwpm4M4_lh19v0Mdz=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn0XJyvuSIB_xL6_itcdEkxrdbw0zThiaVLFXvP49pjgG9dpssRCYa3HH-Y-NeXYwzGXB84nvGI6FYLtihq5rfvZtXk8Zx0hdaAgK84EEvHRznHFUMO4EZcG3kK18ldJKpNrTRf6I2Tzaggr=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3L2SuozpHmjW2SSjOyPzLkX56eYYmcpchcxQKnUKSdA1adtj68N0JhVV9p6wPZ61gtOmx0JhtHfaVgduQlXd0lKePv1k8tWtnJbKKPOICRsB6DauzcPVKFd1urU2PMB2E0YxHYeF3mu8E=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3jyWXqFR7JR_doTpj_DeKZcX2W8J_qSEPeAgj0or5kSPk-yR07dV_VZNJ66jXR96WBLa090_BTmeVBU-LhPM6-ztEe_DT1uTx11o_ChRYMvMMXeeImyibeHnlEXbNF82skzdvVitPgUQjN=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn3z9SwDzzWkNj9pRw7ibtxFrVrTEeOEUWjkBvzHoKfMFSZk-to9WdoOsf37R2ee4D79U1FSPcJ7u-UFh1Nx5C4G1tHsrPqxbnMmCnwn_fCilWWtABO0nUIg0bAqkW8TU7-CBzCs6r8YqNX3=k-no","https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/grass-cs/ANxoTn2_IetFW5mp8FfTH_w7TZmpBGJIRugWfwOnCi2lAjnw3GIc1uND3-zHGn99w1Sf2Ppfk-5SDQGoc1LxFEILN5I6DfN-t34XzbYl5p8FAmkd3uwxkg2bOUSC2HE5eSHLMGPDr1A2y1bwjwEd=k-no"],"When":"2025-11-15"},{"Name":"Manikandan","ProfilePicture":"https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/a-/ALV-UjUK0SkV8TZhYnZSRBKgqRxuLWVvg7jqZPd3mtdwh2dk7IGOOEKr=s120-c-rp-mo-ba12-br100","Rating":5,"Description":"Nebo Fête House in Andheri is a lively and stylish spot that works perfectly for relaxed dinners or celebratory nights out.
